017package org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect;
018
019import static java.util.Collections.*;
020import static java.util.stream.Collectors.*;
021import static java.util.stream.Stream.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.function.Suppliers.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassArrayFormat.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassNameFormat.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AnnotationUtils.*;
026import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
027import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
028import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
029import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.list;
030import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.toList;
031import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
032import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.eq;
033
034import java.lang.annotation.*;
035import java.lang.reflect.*;
036import java.security.*;
037import java.util.*;
038import java.util.function.*;
039import java.util.stream.*;
040
041import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
043
044/**
045 * Lightweight utility class for introspecting information about a class.
046 *
047 * <p>
048 * Provides various convenience methods for introspecting fields/methods/annotations
049 * that aren't provided by the standard Java reflection APIs.
050 *
051 * <p>
052 * Objects are designed to be lightweight to create and threadsafe.
053 *
054 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
055 * <p class='bjava'>
056 *    <jc>// Wrap our class inside a ClassInfo.</jc>
057 *    ClassInfo <jv>classInfo</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
058 *
059 *    <jc>// Get all methods in parent-to-child order, sorted alphabetically per class.</jc>
060 *    <jk>for</jk> (MethodInfo <jv>methodInfo</jv> : <jv>classInfo</jv>.getAllMethods()) {
061 *       <jc>// Do something with it.</jc>
062 *    }
063 *
064 *    <jc>// Get all class-level annotations in parent-to-child order.</jc>
065 *    <jk>for</jk> (MyAnnotation <jv>annotation</jv> : <jv>classInfo</jv>.getAnnotations(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>)) {
066 *       <jc>// Do something with it.</jc>
067 *    }
068 * </p>
069 *
070 */
071@SuppressWarnings({
072   "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
073   "rawtypes", // Raw types necessary for generic type handling
074   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase naming convention
075   "java:S1452"  // Wildcard required - List<AnnotationInfo<? extends Annotation>>, List<TypeVariable<?>>
076})
077public non-sealed class ClassInfo extends ElementInfo implements Annotatable, Type, Comparable<ClassInfo> {
078
079   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
080   private static final String ARG_type = "type";
081   private static final String ARG_pt = "pt";
082
083   private static final Cache<Class,ClassInfo> CACHE = Cache.of(Class.class, ClassInfo.class).build();
084
085   private static final Map<Class<?>,Class<?>> pmap1 = new HashMap<>();
086   private static final Map<Class<?>,Class<?>> pmap2 = new HashMap<>();
087
088   static {
089      pmap1.put(boolean.class, Boolean.class);
090      pmap1.put(byte.class, Byte.class);
091      pmap1.put(short.class, Short.class);
092      pmap1.put(char.class, Character.class);
093      pmap1.put(int.class, Integer.class);
094      pmap1.put(long.class, Long.class);
095      pmap1.put(float.class, Float.class);
096      pmap1.put(double.class, Double.class);
097      pmap2.put(Boolean.class, boolean.class);
098      pmap2.put(Byte.class, byte.class);
099      pmap2.put(Short.class, short.class);
100      pmap2.put(Character.class, char.class);
101      pmap2.put(Integer.class, int.class);
102      pmap2.put(Long.class, long.class);
103      pmap2.put(Float.class, float.class);
104      pmap2.put(Double.class, double.class);
105   }
106
107   // @formatter:off
108   private static final Map<Class,Object> primitiveDefaultMap =
109      mapBuilder(Class.class,Object.class)
110         .unmodifiable()
111         .add(Boolean.TYPE, false)
112         .add(Character.TYPE, (char)0)
113         .add(Short.TYPE, (short)0)
114         .add(Integer.TYPE, 0)
115         .add(Long.TYPE, 0L)
116         .add(Float.TYPE, 0f)
117         .add(Double.TYPE, 0d)
118         .add(Byte.TYPE, (byte)0)
119         .add(Boolean.class, false)
120         .add(Character.class, (char)0)
121         .add(Short.class, (short)0)
122         .add(Integer.class, 0)
123         .add(Long.class, 0L)
124         .add(Float.class, 0f)
125         .add(Double.class, 0d)
126         .add(Byte.class, (byte)0)
127         .build();
128   // @formatter:on
129
130   /**
131    * Returns a class info wrapper around the specified class type.
132    *
133    * @param inner The class type.
134    * @param innerType The generic type (if parameterized type).
135    * @return The constructed class info.
136    */
137   public static ClassInfo of(Class<?> inner, Type innerType) {
138      if (inner == innerType)
139         return of(inner);
140      if (inner != null)
141         return new ClassInfoTyped<>(inner, innerType);
142      return new ClassInfo(null, innerType);
143   }
144
145   /**
146    * Returns a class info wrapper around the specified class type.
147    *
148    * @param <T> The class type.
149    * @param inner The class type.
150    * @return The constructed class info.
151    */
152   public static <T> ClassInfoTyped<T> of(Class<T> inner) {
153      return (ClassInfoTyped<T>) CACHE.get(inner, () -> new ClassInfoTyped<>(inner));
154   }
155
156   /**
157    * Same as using the constructor, but operates on an object instance.
158    *
159    * @param object The class instance.
160    * @return The constructed class info.
161    */
162   public static ClassInfo of(Object object) {
163      return of(object instanceof Class object2 ? (Class<?>)object2 : object.getClass());
164   }
165
166   /**
167    * Returns a class info wrapper around the specified class type.
168    *
169    * @param innerType The class type.
170    * @return The constructed class info.
171    */
172   public static ClassInfo of(Type innerType) {
173      if (innerType instanceof Class<?> c)
174         return of(c);
175      return new ClassInfo(toClass(innerType), innerType);
176   }
177
178   /**
179    * Same as {@link #of(Object)} but attempts to deproxify the object if it's wrapped in a CGLIB proxy.
180    *
181    * @param object The class instance.
182    * @return The constructed class info.
183    */
184   public static ClassInfo ofProxy(Object object) {
185      var inner = getProxyFor(object);
186      return inner == null ? ClassInfo.of(object) : ClassInfo.of(inner);
187   }
188
189   private final Type innerType;  // The underlying Type object (may be Class, ParameterizedType, GenericArrayType, etc.).
190   private final Class<?> inner;  // The underlying Class object (null for non-class types like TypeVariable).
191   private final boolean isParameterizedType;  // True if this represents a ParameterizedType (e.g., List<String>).
192
193   private final Supplier<Integer> dimensions;  // Number of array dimensions (0 if not an array).
194   private final Supplier<ClassInfo> componentType;  // Base component type for arrays (e.g., String for String[][]), also handles GenericArrayType.  Cached and never null.
195   private final Supplier<PackageInfo> packageInfo;  // The package this class belongs to (null for primitive types and arrays).
196   private final Supplier<List<ClassInfo>> parents;  // All superclasses of this class in child-to-parent order, starting with this class.
197   private final Supplier<List<AnnotationInfo>> declaredAnnotations;  // All annotations declared directly on this class, wrapped in AnnotationInfo.
198   private final Supplier<String> nameFull;  // Fully qualified class name with generics (e.g., "java.util.List<java.lang.String>").
199   private final Supplier<String> nameShort;  // Simple class name with generics (e.g., "List<String>").
200   private final Supplier<String> nameReadable;  // Human-readable class name without generics (e.g., "List").
201   private final Supplier<String> toString;  // String representation with modifiers, class type, name, and type parameters.
202   private final Supplier<List<ClassInfo>> declaredInterfaces;  // All interfaces declared directly by this class.
203   private final Supplier<List<ClassInfo>> interfaces;  // All interfaces implemented by this class and its parents, in child-to-parent order.
204   private final Supplier<List<ClassInfo>> allParents;  // All parent classes and interfaces, classes first, then in child-to-parent order.
205   private final Supplier<List<ClassInfo>> parentsAndInterfaces;  // All parent classes and interfaces with proper traversal of interface hierarchy to avoid duplicates.
206   private final Supplier<List<AnnotationInfo<? extends Annotation>>> annotationInfos;  // All annotations on this class and parent classes/interfaces in child-to-parent order.
207   private final Supplier<List<RecordComponent>> recordComponents;  // All record components if this is a record class (Java 14+).
208   private final Supplier<List<Type>> genericInterfaces;  // All generic interface types (e.g., List<String> implements Comparable<List<String>>).
209   private final Supplier<List<TypeVariable<?>>> typeParameters;  // All type parameters declared on this class (e.g., <T, U> in class Foo<T, U>).
210   private final Supplier<List<AnnotatedType>> annotatedInterfaces;  // All annotated interface types with their annotations.
211   private final Supplier<List<Object>> signers;  // All signers of this class (for signed JARs).
212   private final Supplier<List<MethodInfo>> publicMethods;  // All public methods on this class and inherited, excluding Object methods.
213   private final Supplier<List<MethodInfo>> declaredMethods;  // All methods declared directly on this class (public, protected, package, private).
214   private final Supplier<List<MethodInfo>> allMethods;  // All methods from this class and all parents, in child-to-parent order.
215   private final Supplier<List<MethodInfo>> allMethodsTopDown;  // All methods from this class and all parents, in parent-to-child order.
216   private final Supplier<List<FieldInfo>> publicFields;  // All public fields from this class and parents, deduplicated by name (child wins).
217   private final Supplier<List<FieldInfo>> declaredFields;  // All fields declared directly on this class (public, protected, package, private).
218   private final Supplier<List<FieldInfo>> allFields;  // All fields from this class and all parents, in parent-to-child order.
219   private final Supplier<List<ConstructorInfo>> publicConstructors;  // All public constructors declared on this class.
220   private final Supplier<List<ConstructorInfo>> declaredConstructors;  // All constructors declared on this class (public, protected, package, private).
221   private final Supplier<MethodInfo> repeatedAnnotationMethod;  // The repeated annotation method (value()) if this class is a @Repeatable container.
222   private final Cache<Method,MethodInfo> methodCache;  // Cache of wrapped Method objects.
223   private final Cache<Field,FieldInfo> fieldCache;  // Cache of wrapped Field objects.
224   private final Cache<Constructor,ConstructorInfo> constructorCache;  // Cache of wrapped Constructor objects.
225
226   /**
227    * Constructor.
228    *
229    * @param inner The class type.
230    * @param innerType The generic type (if parameterized type).
231    */
232   protected ClassInfo(Class<?> inner, Type innerType) {
233      // @formatter:off
234      super(inner == null ? 0 : inner.getModifiers());
235      assertArg(inner != null || innerType != null, "At least one of inner or innerType must be specified.");
236      this.innerType = innerType;
237      this.inner = inner;
238      this.isParameterizedType = innerType instanceof ParameterizedType;
239      this.dimensions = memoize(this::findDimensions);
240      this.componentType = memoize(this::findComponentType);
241      this.packageInfo = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(Class::getPackage).filter(p -> p != null).map(PackageInfo::of).orElse(null));  // PackageInfo may be null for primitive types and arrays.
242      this.parents = memoize(this::findParents);
243      this.declaredAnnotations = memoize(() -> (List)o(inner).map(x -> u(l(x.getDeclaredAnnotations()))).orElse(emptyList()).stream().flatMap(a -> streamRepeated(a)).map(a -> ai(this, a)).toList());
244      this.nameFull = memoize(() -> getNameFormatted(FULL, true, '$', BRACKETS));
245      this.nameShort = memoize(() -> getNameFormatted(SHORT, true, '$', BRACKETS));
246      this.nameReadable = memoize(() -> getNameFormatted(SIMPLE, false, '$', WORD));
247      this.toString = memoize(this::findToString);
248      this.declaredInterfaces = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(x -> stream(x.getGenericInterfaces()).map(ClassInfo::of).map(ClassInfo.class::cast).toList()).orElse(emptyList()));
249      this.interfaces = memoize(() -> getParents().stream().flatMap(x -> x.getDeclaredInterfaces().stream()).flatMap(ci2 -> concat(Stream.of(ci2), ci2.getInterfaces().stream())).distinct().toList());
250      this.allParents = memoize(() -> concat(getParents().stream(), getInterfaces().stream()).toList());
251      this.parentsAndInterfaces = memoize(this::findParentsAndInterfaces);
252      this.annotationInfos = memoize(this::findAnnotations);
253      this.recordComponents = memoize(() -> o(inner).filter(Class::isRecord).map(x -> u(l(x.getRecordComponents()))).orElse(emptyList()));
254      this.genericInterfaces = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(x -> u(l(x.getGenericInterfaces()))).orElse(emptyList()));
255      this.typeParameters = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(x -> u(l((TypeVariable<?>[])x.getTypeParameters()))).orElse(emptyList()));
256      this.annotatedInterfaces = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(x -> u(l(x.getAnnotatedInterfaces()))).orElse(emptyList()));
257      this.signers = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(Class::getSigners).map(x -> u(l(x))).orElse(emptyList()));
258      this.publicMethods = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(x -> stream(x.getMethods()).filter(m -> neq(m.getDeclaringClass(), Object.class)).map(this::getMethod).sorted().toList()).orElse(emptyList()));
259      this.declaredMethods = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(x -> stream(x.getDeclaredMethods()).filter(m -> neq("$jacocoInit", m.getName())).map(this::getMethod).sorted().toList()).orElse(emptyList()));
260      this.allMethods = memoize(() -> allParents.get().stream().flatMap(c2 -> c2.getDeclaredMethods().stream()).toList());
261      this.allMethodsTopDown = memoize(() -> rstream(getAllParents()).flatMap(c2 -> c2.getDeclaredMethods().stream()).toList());
262      this.publicFields = memoize(() -> parents.get().stream().flatMap(c2 -> c2.getDeclaredFields().stream()).filter(f -> f.isPublic() && neq("$jacocoData", f.getName())).collect(toMap(FieldInfo::getName, x -> x, (a, b) -> a, LinkedHashMap::new)).values().stream().sorted().toList());
263      this.declaredFields = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(x -> stream(x.getDeclaredFields()).filter(f -> neq("$jacocoData", f.getName())).map(this::getField).sorted().toList()).orElse(emptyList()));
264      this.allFields = memoize(() -> rstream(allParents.get()).flatMap(c2 -> c2.getDeclaredFields().stream()).toList());
265      this.publicConstructors = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(x -> stream(x.getConstructors()).map(this::getConstructor).sorted().toList()).orElse(emptyList()));
266      this.declaredConstructors = memoize(() -> o(inner).map(x -> stream(x.getDeclaredConstructors()).map(this::getConstructor).sorted().toList()).orElse(emptyList()));
267      this.repeatedAnnotationMethod = memoize(this::findRepeatedAnnotationMethod);
268      this.methodCache = Cache.of(Method.class, MethodInfo.class).build();
269      this.fieldCache = Cache.of(Field.class, FieldInfo.class).build();
270      this.constructorCache = Cache.of(Constructor.class, ConstructorInfo.class).build();
271      // @formatter:on
272   }
273
274   /**
275    * Appends a formatted class name to a StringBuilder with configurable options.
276    *
277    * <p>
278    * This is the core implementation method used by all other name formatting methods.
279    * Using this method directly avoids String allocations when building complex strings.
280    * The method is recursive to handle nested generic type parameters.
281    *
282    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
283    * <p class='bjava'>
284    *    StringBuilder sb = <jk>new</jk> StringBuilder();
285    *    sb.append(<js>"Class: "</js>);
286    *    ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(HashMap.<jk>class</jk>).appendFormattedName(sb, ClassNameFormat.<jsf>FULL</jsf>, <jk>true</jk>, '$', ClassArrayFormat.<jsf>BRACKETS</jsf>);
287    *    <jc>// sb now contains: "Class: java.util.HashMap"</jc>
288    * </p>
289    *
290    * @param sb
291    *    The StringBuilder to append to.
292    * @param nameFormat
293    *    Controls which parts of the class name to include (package, outer classes).
294    * @param includeTypeParams
295    *    If <jk>true</jk>, include generic type parameters recursively.
296    * @param separator
297    *    Character to use between outer and inner class names.
298    *    <br>Ignored when <c>nameFormat</c> is {@link ClassNameFormat#SIMPLE}.
299    * @param arrayFormat
300    *    How to format array dimensions.
301    * @return
302    *    The same StringBuilder for method chaining.
303    */
304   @SuppressWarnings({
305      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for name formatting logic
306      "java:S6541"  // Synchronization not needed for local StringBuilder operations
307   })
308   public StringBuilder appendNameFormatted(StringBuilder sb, ClassNameFormat nameFormat, boolean includeTypeParams, char separator, ClassArrayFormat arrayFormat) {
309      var dim = getDimensions();
310
311      // Handle arrays - format component type recursively, then add array notation
312      if (dim > 0) {
313         var componentType2 = getComponentType();
314         componentType2.appendNameFormatted(sb, nameFormat, includeTypeParams, separator, arrayFormat);
315
316         if (arrayFormat == ClassArrayFormat.WORD) {
317            for (var i = 0; i < dim; i++)
318               sb.append("Array");
319         } else if (arrayFormat == ClassArrayFormat.BRACKETS) {
320            for (var i = 0; i < dim; i++)
321               sb.append("[]");
322         }
323         // JVM format is already in getName() - would need special handling
324
325         return sb;
326      }
327
328      // Get the raw class - for ParameterizedType, extract the raw type
329      var ct = inner;
330      if (ct == null && isParameterizedType) {
331         var pt = (ParameterizedType)innerType;
332         ct = (Class<?>)pt.getRawType();
333      }
334
335      // Append base class name based on format
336      switch (nameFormat) {
337         case FULL:
338            // Full package name + outer classes
339            if (nn(ct)) {
340               sb.append(ct.getName());
341               // Apply separator if not '$'
342               if (separator != '$' && sb.indexOf("$") != -1) {
343                  for (var i = 0; i < sb.length(); i++) {
344                     if (sb.charAt(i) == '$')
345                        sb.setCharAt(i, separator);
346                  }
347               }
348            } else {
349               sb.append(innerType.getTypeName());
350            }
351            break;
352
353         case SHORT:
354            // Outer classes but no package
355            if (nn(ct)) {
356               if (ct.isLocalClass()) {
357                  // Local class: recursively include all enclosing classes
358                  appendShortNameWithOuters(sb, of(ct.getEnclosingClass()), separator).append(separator).append(ct.getSimpleName());
359               } else if (ct.isMemberClass()) {
360                  // Member class: recursively include all declaring classes
361                  appendShortNameWithOuters(sb, of(ct.getDeclaringClass()), separator).append(separator).append(ct.getSimpleName());
362               } else {
363                  // Regular class: just simple name
364                  sb.append(ct.getSimpleName());
365               }
366            } else {
367               sb.append(innerType.getTypeName());
368            }
369            break;
370
371         default /* SIMPLE */:
372            // Simple name only - no package, no outer classes
373            if (nn(ct)) {
374               sb.append(ct.getSimpleName());
375            } else {
376               sb.append(innerType.getTypeName());
377            }
378            break;
379      }
380
381      // Append type parameters if requested
382      if (includeTypeParams && isParameterizedType) {
383         var pt = (ParameterizedType)innerType;
384         sb.append('<');
385         var first = true;
386         for (var t2 : pt.getActualTypeArguments()) {
387            if (! first)
388               sb.append(',');
389            first = false;
390            of(t2).appendNameFormatted(sb, nameFormat, includeTypeParams, separator, arrayFormat);
391         }
392         sb.append('>');
393      }
394
395      return sb;
396   }
397
398   /**
399    * Helper method to recursively append short name with all outer classes for SHORT format.
400    * This ensures multiply-nested classes include all outer class names.
401    *
402    * @param sb The StringBuilder to append to.
403    * @param classInfo The ClassInfo to append.
404    * @param separator The separator character between outer and inner classes.
405    */
406   private StringBuilder appendShortNameWithOuters(StringBuilder sb, ClassInfo classInfo, char separator) {
407      var ct = classInfo.inner();
408      if (ct == null) {
409         sb.append(classInfo.getNameSimple());
410         return sb;
411      }
412
413      if (ct.isLocalClass()) {
414         appendShortNameWithOuters(sb, of(ct.getEnclosingClass()), separator).append(separator).append(ct.getSimpleName());
415      } else if (ct.isMemberClass()) {
416         appendShortNameWithOuters(sb, of(ct.getDeclaringClass()), separator).append(separator).append(ct.getSimpleName());
417      } else {
418         sb.append(ct.getSimpleName());
419      }
420      return sb;
421   }
422
423   /**
424    * Returns a {@link ClassInfo} for an array type whose component type is this class.
425    *
426    * @return A {@link ClassInfo} representing an array type whose component type is this class.
427    */
428   public ClassInfo arrayType() {
429      if (inner == null)
430         return null;
431      return of(inner.arrayType());
432   }
433
434   /**
435    * Casts this {@link ClassInfo} object to represent a subclass of the class represented by the specified class object.
436    *
437    * @param <U> The type to cast to.
438    * @param clazz The class of the type to cast to.
439    * @return This {@link ClassInfo} object, cast to represent a subclass of the specified class object.
440    * @throws ClassCastException If this class is not assignable to the specified class.
441    */
442   public <U> ClassInfo asSubclass(Class<U> clazz) {
443      if (inner == null)
444         return null;
445      inner.asSubclass(clazz);  // Throws ClassCastException if not assignable
446      return this;
447   }
448
449   /**
450    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this type can be used as a parameter for the specified object.
451    *
452    * <p>
453    * For null values, returns <jk>true</jk> unless this type is a primitive
454    * (since primitives cannot accept null values in Java).
455    *
456    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
457    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
458    *    <li><c>ClassInfo.of(String.class).canAcceptArg("foo")</c> - returns <jk>true</jk>
459    *    <li><c>ClassInfo.of(String.class).canAcceptArg(null)</c> - returns <jk>true</jk>
460    *    <li><c>ClassInfo.of(int.class).canAcceptArg(5)</c> - returns <jk>true</jk>
461    *    <li><c>ClassInfo.of(int.class).canAcceptArg(null)</c> - returns <jk>false</jk> (primitives can't be null)
462    *    <li><c>ClassInfo.of(Integer.class).canAcceptArg(null)</c> - returns <jk>true</jk>
463    * </ul>
464    *
465    * @param child The argument to check.
466    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this type can be used as a parameter for the specified object.
467    */
468   public boolean canAcceptArg(Object child) {
469      if (inner == null)
470         return false;
471      if (child == null)
472         return ! isPrimitive();  // Primitives can't accept null, all other types can
473      if (inner.isInstance(child))
474         return true;
475      if (this.isPrimitive())
476         return this.getWrapperIfPrimitive().isAssignableFrom(of(child).getWrapperIfPrimitive());
477      return false;
478   }
479
480   /**
481    * Casts an object to the class represented by this {@link ClassInfo} object.
482    *
483    * @param <T> The type to cast to.
484    * @param obj The object to be cast.
485    * @return The object after casting, or <jk>null</jk> if obj is <jk>null</jk>.
486    * @throws ClassCastException If the object is not <jk>null</jk> and is not assignable to this class.
487    */
488   public <T> T cast(Object obj) {
489      return inner == null ? null : (T)inner.cast(obj);
490   }
491
492   /**
493    * Returns the component type of this class if it is an array type.
494    *
495    * <p>
496    * This is equivalent to {@link Class#getComponentType()} but returns a {@link ClassInfo} instead.
497    * Note that {@link #getComponentType()} also exists and returns the base component type for multi-dimensional arrays.
498    *
499    * @return The {@link ClassInfo} representing the component type, or <jk>null</jk> if this class does not represent an array type.
500    */
501   public ClassInfo componentType() {
502      if (inner == null)
503         return null;
504      var ct = inner.componentType();
505      return ct == null ? null : of(ct);
506   }
507
508   /**
509    * Returns the descriptor string of this class.
510    *
511    * <p>
512    * The descriptor is a string representing the type of the class, as specified in JVMS 4.3.2.
513    * For example, the descriptor for <c>String</c> is <js>"Ljava/lang/String;"</js>.
514    *
515    * @return The descriptor string of this class.
516    */
517   public String descriptorString() {
518      return inner == null ? null : inner.descriptorString();
519   }
520
521   @Override
522   public boolean equals(Object o) {
523      return (o instanceof ClassInfo o2) && eq(this, o2, (x, y) -> eq(x.innerType, y.innerType));
524   }
525
526   @Override
527   public int compareTo(ClassInfo o) {
528      if (o == null)
529         return 1;
530      return cmp(getName(), o.getName());
531   }
532
533   /**
534    * Returns all fields on this class and all parent classes.
535    *
536    * <p>
537    *    Results are ordered parent-to-child, and then alphabetical per class.
538    *
539    * @return
540    *    All declared fields on this class.
541    *    <br>List is unmodifiable.
542    */
543   public List<FieldInfo> getAllFields() { return allFields.get(); }
544
545   /**
546    * Returns all declared methods on this class and all parent classes in <b>child-to-parent order</b>.
547    *
548    * <p>
549    * This method returns methods of <b>all visibility levels</b> (public, protected, package-private, and private).
550    *
551    * <p>
552    * Methods are returned in <b>child-to-parent order</b> - methods from the current class appear first,
553    * followed by methods from the immediate parent, then grandparent, etc. Within each class, methods
554    * are sorted alphabetically.
555    *
556    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
557    * <p class='bjava'>
558    *    <jc>// Given class hierarchy:</jc>
559    *    <jk>class</jk> Parent {
560    *       <jk>void</jk> method1() {}
561    *       <jk>void</jk> method2() {}
562    *    }
563    *    <jk>class</jk> Child <jk>extends</jk> Parent {
564    *       <jk>void</jk> method3() {}
565    *       <jk>void</jk> method4() {}
566    *    }
567    *
568    *    <jc>// getAllMethods() returns in child-to-parent order:</jc>
569    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(Child.<jk>class</jk>);
570    *    List&lt;MethodInfo&gt; <jv>methods</jv> = <jv>ci</jv>.getAllMethods();
571    *    <jc>// Returns: [method3, method4, method1, method2]</jc>
572    *    <jc>//          ^Child methods^   ^Parent methods^</jc>
573    * </p>
574    *
575    * <h5 class='section'>Comparison with Similar Methods:</h5>
576    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
577    *    <li>{@link #getDeclaredMethods()} - Returns methods declared on this class only (all visibility levels)
578    *    <li>{@link #getAllMethods()} - Returns all methods in <b>child-to-parent order</b> ← This method
579    *    <li>{@link #getAllMethodsTopDown()} - Returns all methods in <b>parent-to-child order</b>
580    *    <li>{@link #getPublicMethods()} - Returns public methods only on this class and parents
581    * </ul>
582    *
583    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5>
584    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
585    *    <li>Unlike Java's {@link Class#getMethods()}, this returns methods of all visibility levels, not just public ones.
586    *    <li>Methods from {@link Object} class are excluded from the results.
587    *    <li>Use {@link #getAllMethodsTopDown()} if you need parent methods to appear before child methods.
588    * </ul>
589    *
590    * @return
591    *    All declared methods on this class and all parent classes (all visibility levels).
592    *    <br>Results are ordered child-to-parent, and then alphabetically per class.
593    *    <br>List is unmodifiable.
594    */
595   public List<MethodInfo> getAllMethods() { return allMethods.get(); }
596
597   /**
598    * Returns all declared methods on this class and all parent classes in <b>parent-to-child order</b>.
599    *
600    * <p>
601    * This method returns methods of <b>all visibility levels</b> (public, protected, package-private, and private).
602    *
603    * <p>
604    * Methods are returned in <b>parent-to-child order</b> - methods from the most distant ancestor appear first,
605    * followed by methods from each subsequent child class, ending with methods from the current class. Within
606    * each class, methods are sorted alphabetically.
607    *
608    * <p>
609    * This ordering is useful for initialization hooks and lifecycle methods where parent methods should execute
610    * before child methods (e.g., <c>@RestInit</c>, <c>@PostConstruct</c>, etc.).
611    *
612    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
613    * <p class='bjava'>
614    *    <jc>// Given class hierarchy:</jc>
615    *    <jk>class</jk> Parent {
616    *       <jk>void</jk> method1() {}
617    *       <jk>void</jk> method2() {}
618    *    }
619    *    <jk>class</jk> Child <jk>extends</jk> Parent {
620    *       <jk>void</jk> method3() {}
621    *       <jk>void</jk> method4() {}
622    *    }
623    *
624    *    <jc>// getAllMethodsParentFirst() returns in parent-to-child order:</jc>
625    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(Child.<jk>class</jk>);
626    *    List&lt;MethodInfo&gt; <jv>methods</jv> = <jv>ci</jv>.getAllMethodsParentFirst();
627    *    <jc>// Returns: [method1, method2, method3, method4]</jc>
628    *    <jc>//          ^Parent methods^  ^Child methods^</jc>
629    * </p>
630    *
631    * <h5 class='section'>Comparison with Similar Methods:</h5>
632    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
633    *    <li>{@link #getDeclaredMethods()} - Returns methods declared on this class only (all visibility levels)
634    *    <li>{@link #getAllMethods()} - Returns all methods in <b>child-to-parent order</b>
635    *    <li>{@link #getAllMethodsTopDown()} - Returns all methods in <b>parent-to-child order</b> ← This method
636    *    <li>{@link #getPublicMethods()} - Returns public methods only on this class and parents
637    * </ul>
638    *
639    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5>
640    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
641    *    <li>Methods from {@link Object} class are excluded from the results.
642    *    <li>Use {@link #getAllMethods()} if you need child methods to appear before parent methods.
643    * </ul>
644    *
645    * @return
646    *    All declared methods on this class and all parent classes (all visibility levels).
647    *    <br>Results are ordered parent-to-child, and then alphabetically per class.
648    *    <br>List is unmodifiable.
649    */
650   public List<MethodInfo> getAllMethodsTopDown() { return allMethodsTopDown.get(); }
651
652   /**
653    * Returns a list including this class and all parent classes and interfaces.
654    *
655    * <p>
656    * Results are classes-before-interfaces, then child-to-parent order.
657    *
658    * @return An unmodifiable list including this class and all parent classes.
659    *    <br>Results are ordered child-to-parent order with classes listed before interfaces.
660    */
661   public List<ClassInfo> getAllParents() { return allParents.get(); }
662
663   @Override /* Annotatable */
664   public AnnotatableType getAnnotatableType() { return AnnotatableType.CLASS_TYPE; }
665
666   /**
667    * Returns a list of {@link AnnotatedType} objects that represent the annotated interfaces
668    * implemented by this class.
669    *
670    * <p>
671    * Returns a cached, unmodifiable list.
672    * If this class represents a class or interface whose superinterfaces are annotated,
673    * the returned objects reflect the annotations used in the source code to declare the superinterfaces.
674    *
675    * @return
676    *    An unmodifiable list of {@link AnnotatedType} objects representing the annotated superinterfaces.
677    *    <br>Returns an empty list if this class implements no interfaces.
678    */
679   public List<AnnotatedType> getAnnotatedInterfaces() { return annotatedInterfaces.get(); }
680
681   /**
682    * Returns an {@link AnnotatedType} object that represents the annotated superclass of this class.
683    *
684    * <p>
685    * If this class represents a class type whose superclass is annotated, the returned object reflects
686    * the annotations used in the source code to declare the superclass.
687    *
688    * @return
689    *    An {@link AnnotatedType} object representing the annotated superclass,
690    *    or <jk>null</jk> if this class represents {@link Object}, an interface, a primitive type, or void.
691    */
692   public AnnotatedType getAnnotatedSuperclass() { return inner == null ? null : inner.getAnnotatedSuperclass(); }
693
694   /**
695    * Returns all annotations on this class and parent classes/interfaces in child-to-parent order.
696    *
697    * <p>
698    * This returns all declared annotations from:
699    * <ol>
700    *    <li>This class
701    *    <li>Parent classes in child-to-parent order
702    *    <li>For each class, interfaces declared on that class and their parent interfaces
703    *    <li>The package of this class
704    * </ol>
705    *
706    * <p>
707    * This does NOT include runtime annotations. For runtime annotation support, use
708    * {@link AnnotationProvider}.
709    *
710    * @return An unmodifiable list of all annotation infos.
711    */
712   public List<AnnotationInfo<? extends Annotation>> getAnnotations() { return annotationInfos.get(); }
713
714   /**
715    * Returns all annotations of the specified type on this class and parent classes/interfaces in child-to-parent order.
716    *
717    * <p>
718    * This returns all declared annotations from:
719    * <ol>
720    *    <li>This class
721    *    <li>Parent classes in child-to-parent order
722    *    <li>For each class, interfaces declared on that class and their parent interfaces
723    *    <li>The package of this class
724    * </ol>
725    *
726    * <p>
727    * This does NOT include runtime annotations. For runtime annotation support, use
728    * {@link AnnotationProvider}.
729    *
730    * @param <A> The annotation type.
731    * @param type The annotation type to filter by.
732    * @return A stream of annotation infos of the specified type.
733    */
734   public <A extends Annotation> Stream<AnnotationInfo<A>> getAnnotations(Class<A> type) {
735      assertArgNotNull(ARG_type, type);
736      return getAnnotations().stream().filter(a -> a.isType(type)).map(a -> (AnnotationInfo<A>)a);
737   }
738
739   /**
740    * Returns the {@link ClassLoader} for this class.
741    *
742    * <p>
743    * If this class represents a primitive type or void, <jk>null</jk> is returned.
744    *
745    * @return The class loader for this class, or <jk>null</jk> if it doesn't have one.
746    */
747   public ClassLoader getClassLoader() { return inner == null ? null : inner.getClassLoader(); }
748
749   /**
750    * Returns the base component type of this class.
751    *
752    * <p>
753    * For array types (e.g., <c>String[][]</c>), returns the deepest component type (e.g., <c>String</c>).
754    * <br>For non-array types, returns this class itself.
755    *
756    * <p>
757    * <b>Note:</b> Unlike {@link Class#getComponentType()}, this method also handles generic array types (e.g., <c>List&lt;String&gt;[]</c>)
758    * and returns the full parameterized type information (e.g., <c>List&lt;String&gt;</c>).
759    * Additionally, this method never returns <jk>null</jk> - non-array types return <jk>this</jk> instead.
760    *
761    * @return The base component type of an array, or this class if not an array.
762    */
763   public ClassInfo getComponentType() { return componentType.get(); }
764
765   /**
766    * Returns all annotations declared directly on this class, wrapped in {@link AnnotationInfo} objects.
767    *
768    * <p>
769    * This includes annotations explicitly applied to the class declaration, but excludes inherited annotations
770    * from parent classes. Each annotation is wrapped for additional functionality such as annotation member
771    * access and metadata inspection.
772    *
773    * <p>
774    * <b>Note on Repeatable Annotations:</b>
775    * Repeatable annotations (those marked with {@link Repeatable @Repeatable}) are automatically
776    * expanded into their individual annotation instances. For example, if a class has multiple {@code @Marshalled} annotations,
777    * this method returns each {@code @Marshalled} annotation separately, rather than the container annotation.
778    *
779    * @return
780    *    An unmodifiable list of {@link AnnotationInfo} wrappers for annotations declared directly on this class.
781    *    <br>List is empty if no annotations are declared.
782    *    <br>Results are in declaration order.
783    *    <br>Repeatable annotations are expanded into individual instances.
784    */
785   public List<AnnotationInfo> getDeclaredAnnotations() { return declaredAnnotations.get(); }
786
787   /**
788    * Returns the first matching declared constructor on this class.
789    *
790    * @param filter A predicate to apply to the entries to determine if value should be used.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
791    * @return The declared constructor that matches the specified predicate.
792    */
793   public Optional<ConstructorInfo> getDeclaredConstructor(Predicate<ConstructorInfo> filter) {
794      return declaredConstructors.get().stream().filter(x -> t(filter, x)).findFirst();
795   }
796
797   /**
798    * Returns all the constructors defined on this class.
799    *
800    * @return
801    *    All constructors defined on this class.
802    *    <br>List is unmodifiable.
803    */
804   public List<ConstructorInfo> getDeclaredConstructors() { return declaredConstructors.get(); }
805
806   /**
807    * Returns the first matching declared field on this class.
808    *
809    * @param filter A predicate to apply to the entries to determine if value should be used.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
810    * @return The declared field, or <jk>null</jk> if not found.
811    */
812   public Optional<FieldInfo> getDeclaredField(Predicate<FieldInfo> filter) {
813      return declaredFields.get().stream().filter(x -> t(filter, x)).findFirst();
814   }
815
816   /**
817    * Returns all declared fields on this class.
818    *
819    * @return
820    *    All declared fields on this class.
821    *    <br>Results are in alphabetical order.
822    *    <br>List is unmodifiable.
823    */
824   public List<FieldInfo> getDeclaredFields() { return declaredFields.get(); }
825
826   /**
827    * Returns a list of interfaces declared on this class.
828    *
829    * <p>
830    * Does not include interfaces declared on parent classes.
831    *
832    * <p>
833    * Results are in the same order as Class.getInterfaces().
834    *
835    * @return
836    *    An unmodifiable list of interfaces declared on this class.
837    *    <br>Results are in the same order as {@link Class#getInterfaces()}.
838    */
839   public List<ClassInfo> getDeclaredInterfaces() { return declaredInterfaces.get(); }
840
841   /**
842    * Returns all classes and interfaces declared as members of this class.
843    *
844    * <p>
845    * This includes public, protected, default (package) access, and private classes and interfaces
846    * declared by the class, but excludes inherited classes and interfaces.
847    *
848    * @return
849    *    An unmodifiable list of all classes and interfaces declared as members of this class.
850    *    <br>Returns an empty list if this class declares no classes or interfaces as members.
851    */
852   public List<ClassInfo> getDeclaredMemberClasses() {
853      if (inner == null)
854         return u(l());
855      Class<?>[] classes = inner.getDeclaredClasses();
856      List<ClassInfo> l = listOfSize(classes.length);
857      for (Class<?> cc : classes)
858         l.add(of(cc));
859      return u(l);
860   }
861
862   /**
863    * Returns the first matching declared method on this class.
864    *
865    * @param filter A predicate to apply to the entries to determine if value should be used.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
866    * @return The first matching method, or <jk>null</jk> if no methods matched.
867    */
868   public Optional<MethodInfo> getDeclaredMethod(Predicate<MethodInfo> filter) {
869      return declaredMethods.get().stream().filter(x -> t(filter, x)).findFirst();
870   }
871
872   /**
873    * Returns all methods declared on this class.
874    *
875    * <p>
876    * This method returns methods of <b>all visibility levels</b> (public, protected, package-private, and private)
877    * declared directly on this class only (does not include inherited methods).
878    *
879    * <h5 class='section'>Comparison with Similar Methods:</h5>
880    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
881    *    <li>{@link #getDeclaredMethods()} - Returns all declared methods on this class only (all visibility levels) ← This method
882    *    <li>{@link #getAllMethods()} - Returns all declared methods on this class and parents (all visibility levels)
883    *    <li>{@link #getPublicMethods()} - Returns public methods only on this class and parents
884    * </ul>
885    *
886    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5>
887    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
888    *    <li>Unlike Java's {@link Class#getDeclaredMethods()}, results are filtered to exclude synthetic methods like <c>$jacocoInit</c>.
889    * </ul>
890    *
891    * @return
892    *    All methods declared on this class (all visibility levels).
893    *    <br>Results are ordered alphabetically.
894    *    <br>List is unmodifiable.
895    */
896   public List<MethodInfo> getDeclaredMethods() { return declaredMethods.get(); }
897
898   /**
899    * Returns the {@link Class} object representing the class or interface that declares the member class
900    * represented by this class.
901    *
902    * <p>
903    * This method returns the class in which this class is explicitly declared as a member.
904    * It only returns non-null for <b>member classes</b> (static and non-static nested classes).
905    *
906    * <p>
907    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
908    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
909    *    <li><c>class Outer { class Inner {} }</c> - <c>Inner.getDeclaringClass()</c> returns <c>Outer</c>
910    *    <li><c>class Outer { static class Nested {} }</c> - <c>Nested.getDeclaringClass()</c> returns <c>Outer</c>
911    *    <li><c>class Outer { void method() { class Local {} } }</c> - <c>Local.getDeclaringClass()</c> returns <jk>null</jk>
912    *    <li>Top-level class - <c>getDeclaringClass()</c> returns <jk>null</jk>
913    *    <li>Anonymous class - <c>getDeclaringClass()</c> returns <jk>null</jk>
914    * </ul>
915    *
916    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
917    *    <li class='ja'>{@link #getEnclosingClass()} - Returns the immediately enclosing class (works for local and anonymous classes too)
918    * </ul>
919    *
920    * @return The declaring class, or <jk>null</jk> if this class is not a member of another class.
921    */
922   public ClassInfo getDeclaringClass() {
923      if (inner == null)
924         return null;
925      var dc = inner.getDeclaringClass();
926      return dc == null ? null : of(dc);
927   }
928
929   /**
930    * Returns the number of dimensions if this is an array type.
931    *
932    * @return The number of dimensions if this is an array type, or <c>0</c> if it is not.
933    */
934   public int getDimensions() { return dimensions.get(); }
935
936   /**
937    * Returns the immediately enclosing class of this class.
938    *
939    * <p>
940    * This method returns the lexically enclosing class, regardless of whether this class is a member,
941    * local, or anonymous class. Unlike {@link #getDeclaringClass()}, this method works for all types of nested classes.
942    *
943    * <p>
944    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
945    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
946    *    <li><c>class Outer { class Inner {} }</c> - <c>Inner.getEnclosingClass()</c> returns <c>Outer</c>
947    *    <li><c>class Outer { static class Nested {} }</c> - <c>Nested.getEnclosingClass()</c> returns <c>Outer</c>
948    *    <li><c>class Outer { void method() { class Local {} } }</c> - <c>Local.getEnclosingClass()</c> returns <c>Outer</c>
949    *    <li><c>class Outer { void method() { new Runnable() {...} } }</c> - Anonymous class <c>getEnclosingClass()</c> returns <c>Outer</c>
950    *    <li>Top-level class - <c>getEnclosingClass()</c> returns <jk>null</jk>
951    * </ul>
952    *
953    * <h5 class='section'>Differences from {@link #getDeclaringClass()}:</h5>
954    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
955    *    <li><c>getDeclaringClass()</c> - Returns non-null only for member classes (static or non-static nested classes)
956    *    <li><c>getEnclosingClass()</c> - Returns non-null for all nested classes (member, local, and anonymous)
957    * </ul>
958    *
959    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
960    *    <li class='ja'>{@link #getDeclaringClass()} - Returns the declaring class (only for member classes)
961    *    <li class='ja'>{@link #getEnclosingConstructor()} - Returns the enclosing constructor (for classes defined in constructors)
962    *    <li class='ja'>{@link #getEnclosingMethod()} - Returns the enclosing method (for classes defined in methods)
963    * </ul>
964    *
965    * @return The enclosing class, or <jk>null</jk> if this is a top-level class.
966    */
967   public ClassInfo getEnclosingClass() {
968      if (inner == null)
969         return null;
970      var ec = inner.getEnclosingClass();
971      return ec == null ? null : of(ec);
972   }
973
974   /**
975    * Returns the {@link ConstructorInfo} object representing the constructor that declares this class if this is a
976    * local or anonymous class declared within a constructor.
977    *
978    * <p>
979    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if this class was not declared within a constructor.
980    *
981    * @return The enclosing constructor, or <jk>null</jk> if this class was not declared within a constructor.
982    */
983   public ConstructorInfo getEnclosingConstructor() {
984      if (inner == null)
985         return null;
986      Constructor<?> ec = inner.getEnclosingConstructor();
987      return ec == null ? null : getConstructor(ec);
988   }
989
990   /**
991    * Returns the {@link MethodInfo} object representing the method that declares this class if this is a
992    * local or anonymous class declared within a method.
993    *
994    * <p>
995    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if this class was not declared within a method.
996    *
997    * @return The enclosing method, or <jk>null</jk> if this class was not declared within a method.
998    */
999   public MethodInfo getEnclosingMethod() {
1000      if (inner == null)
1001         return null;
1002      var em = inner.getEnclosingMethod();
1003      return em == null ? null : getMethod(em);
1004   }
1005
1006   /**
1007    * Returns the {@link Type}s representing the interfaces directly implemented by this class.
1008    *
1009    * <p>
1010    * Returns a cached, unmodifiable list.
1011    * If a superinterface is a parameterized type, the {@link Type} returned for it reflects the actual
1012    * type parameters used in the source code.
1013    *
1014    * @return
1015    *    An unmodifiable list of {@link Type}s representing the interfaces directly implemented by this class.
1016    *    <br>Returns an empty list if this class implements no interfaces.
1017    */
1018   public List<Type> getGenericInterfaces() { return genericInterfaces.get(); }
1019
1020   /**
1021    * Returns the {@link Type} representing the direct superclass of this class.
1022    *
1023    * <p>
1024    * If the superclass is a parameterized type, the {@link Type} returned reflects the actual
1025    * type parameters used in the source code.
1026    *
1027    * @return
1028    *    The superclass of this class as a {@link Type},
1029    *    or <jk>null</jk> if this class represents {@link Object}, an interface, a primitive type, or void.
1030    */
1031   public Type getGenericSuperclass() { return inner == null ? null : inner.getGenericSuperclass(); }
1032
1033   /**
1034    * Returns a list of interfaces defined on this class and superclasses.
1035    *
1036    * <p>
1037    * Results are in child-to-parent order.
1038    *
1039    * @return
1040    *    An unmodifiable list of interfaces defined on this class and superclasses.
1041    *    <br>Results are in child-to-parent order.
1042    */
1043   public List<ClassInfo> getInterfaces() { return interfaces.get(); }
1044
1045   @Override /* Annotatable */
1046   public String getLabel() { return getNameSimple(); }
1047
1048   /**
1049    * Returns all public member classes and interfaces declared by this class and its superclasses.
1050    *
1051    * <p>
1052    * This includes public class and interface members inherited from superclasses and
1053    * public class and interface members declared by the class.
1054    *
1055    * @return
1056    *    An unmodifiable list of all public member classes and interfaces declared by this class.
1057    *    <br>Returns an empty list if this class has no public member classes or interfaces.
1058    */
1059   public List<ClassInfo> getMemberClasses() {
1060      if (inner == null)
1061         return u(l());
1062      Class<?>[] classes = inner.getClasses();
1063      List<ClassInfo> l = listOfSize(classes.length);
1064      for (Class<?> cc : classes)
1065         l.add(of(cc));
1066      return u(l);
1067   }
1068
1069   /**
1070    * Returns the first matching method on this class.
1071    *
1072    * @param filter A predicate to apply to the entries to determine if value should be used.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1073    * @return The first matching method, or <jk>null</jk> if no methods matched.
1074    */
1075   public Optional<MethodInfo> getMethod(Predicate<MethodInfo> filter) {
1076      return allMethods.get().stream().filter(x -> t(filter, x)).findFirst();
1077   }
1078
1079   /**
1080    * Returns the module that this class is a member of.
1081    *
1082    * <p>
1083    * If this class is not in a named module, returns the unnamed module of the class loader for this class.
1084    *
1085    * @return The module that this class is a member of.
1086    */
1087   public Module getModule() { return inner == null ? null : inner.getModule(); }
1088
1089   /**
1090    * Returns the name of the underlying class.
1091    *
1092    * <p>
1093    * Equivalent to calling {@link Class#getName()} or {@link Type#getTypeName()} depending on whether
1094    * this is a class or type.
1095    *
1096    * <p>
1097    * This method returns the JVM internal format for class names:
1098    * <ul>
1099    *    <li>Uses fully qualified package names
1100    *    <li>Uses <js>'$'</js> separator for nested classes
1101    *    <li>Uses JVM notation for arrays (e.g., <js>"[Ljava.lang.String;"</js>)
1102    *    <li>Uses single letters for primitive arrays (e.g., <js>"[I"</js> for <c>int[]</c>)
1103    * </ul>
1104    *
1105    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
1106    * <ul>
1107    *    <li><js>"java.lang.String"</js> - Normal class
1108    *    <li><js>"[Ljava.lang.String;"</js> - Array
1109    *    <li><js>"[[Ljava.lang.String;"</js> - Multi-dimensional array
1110    *    <li><js>"java.util.Map$Entry"</js> - Nested class
1111    *    <li><js>"int"</js> - Primitive class
1112    *    <li><js>"[I"</js> - Primitive array
1113    *    <li><js>"[[I"</js> - Multi-dimensional primitive array
1114    * </ul>
1115    *
1116    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5>
1117    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
1118    *    <li><c>{@link #getNameCanonical()}</c> - Java source code format (uses <js>'.'</js> and <js>"[]"</js>)
1119    *    <li><c>{@link #getNameSimple()}</c> - Simple class name without package
1120    *    <li><c>{@link #getNameFull()}</c> - Full name with type parameters
1121    *    <li><c>{@link #getNameShort()}</c> - Short name with type parameters
1122    *    <li><c>{@link #getNameReadable()}</c> - Human-readable name (uses <js>"Array"</js> suffix)
1123    * </ul>
1124    *
1125    * @return The name of the underlying class in JVM format.
1126    */
1127   public String getName() { return nn(inner) ? inner.getName() : innerType.getTypeName(); }
1128
1129   /**
1130    * Returns the canonical name of the underlying class.
1131    *
1132    * <p>
1133    * The canonical name is the name that would be used in Java source code to refer to the class.
1134    * For example:
1135    * <ul>
1136    *    <li><js>"java.lang.String"</js> - Normal class
1137    *    <li><js>"java.lang.String[]"</js> - Array
1138    *    <li><js>"java.util.Map.Entry"</js> - Nested class
1139    *    <li><jk>null</jk> - Local or anonymous class
1140    * </ul>
1141    *
1142    * @return The canonical name of the underlying class, or <jk>null</jk> if this class doesn't have a canonical name.
1143    */
1144   public String getNameCanonical() {
1145      // For Class objects, delegate to Class.getCanonicalName() which handles local/anonymous classes
1146      // For Type objects (ParameterizedType, etc.), compute the canonical name
1147      if (inner != null && ! isParameterizedType) {
1148         return inner.getCanonicalName();
1149      }
1150      // For ParameterizedType, we can't have a true canonical name with type parameters
1151      // Return null to maintain consistency with Class.getCanonicalName() behavior
1152      return null;
1153   }
1154
1155   /**
1156    * Returns a formatted class name with configurable options.
1157    *
1158    * <p>
1159    * This is a unified method that can produce output equivalent to all other name methods
1160    * by varying the parameters.
1161    *
1162    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
1163    * <p class='bjava'>
1164    *    <jc>// Given: java.util.HashMap&lt;String,Integer&gt;[]</jc>
1165    *
1166    *    <jc>// Full name with generics</jc>
1167    *    getFormattedName(ClassNameFormat.<jsf>FULL</jsf>, <jk>true</jk>, '$', ClassArrayFormat.<jsf>BRACKETS</jsf>)
1168    *    <jc>// → "java.util.HashMap&lt;java.lang.String,java.lang.Integer&gt;[]"</jc>
1169    *
1170    *    <jc>// Short name with generics</jc>
1171    *    getFormattedName(ClassNameFormat.<jsf>SHORT</jsf>, <jk>true</jk>, '$', ClassArrayFormat.<jsf>BRACKETS</jsf>)
1172    *    <jc>// → "HashMap&lt;String,Integer&gt;[]"</jc>
1173    *
1174    *    <jc>// Simple name</jc>
1175    *    getFormattedName(ClassNameFormat.<jsf>SIMPLE</jsf>, <jk>false</jk>, '$', ClassArrayFormat.<jsf>BRACKETS</jsf>)
1176    *    <jc>// → "HashMap[]"</jc>
1177    *
1178    *    <jc>// With dot separator</jc>
1179    *    getFormattedName(ClassNameFormat.<jsf>SHORT</jsf>, <jk>false</jk>, '.', ClassArrayFormat.<jsf>BRACKETS</jsf>)
1180    *    <jc>// → "Map.Entry"</jc>
1181    *
1182    *    <jc>// Word format for arrays</jc>
1183    *    getFormattedName(ClassNameFormat.<jsf>SIMPLE</jsf>, <jk>false</jk>, '$', ClassArrayFormat.<jsf>WORD</jsf>)
1184    *    <jc>// → "HashMapArray"</jc>
1185    * </p>
1186    *
1187    * <h5 class='section'>Equivalent Methods:</h5>
1188    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
1189    *    <li><c>getName()</c> = <c>getFormattedName(FULL, <jk>false</jk>, '$', JVM)</c>
1190    *    <li><c>getCanonicalName()</c> = <c>getFormattedName(FULL, <jk>false</jk>, '.', BRACKETS)</c>
1191    *    <li><c>getSimpleName()</c> = <c>getFormattedName(SIMPLE, <jk>false</jk>, '$', BRACKETS)</c>
1192    *    <li><c>getFullName()</c> = <c>getFormattedName(FULL, <jk>true</jk>, '$', BRACKETS)</c>
1193    *    <li><c>getShortName()</c> = <c>getFormattedName(SHORT, <jk>true</jk>, '$', BRACKETS)</c>
1194    *    <li><c>getReadableName()</c> = <c>getFormattedName(SIMPLE, <jk>false</jk>, '$', WORD)</c>
1195    * </ul>
1196    *
1197    * @param nameFormat
1198    *    Controls which parts of the class name to include (package, outer classes).
1199    * @param includeTypeParams
1200    *    If <jk>true</jk>, include generic type parameters recursively.
1201    *    <br>For example: <js>"HashMap&lt;String,Integer&gt;"</js> instead of <js>"HashMap"</js>
1202    * @param separator
1203    *    Character to use between outer and inner class names.
1204    *    <br>Typically <js>'$'</js> (JVM format) or <js>'.'</js> (canonical format).
1205    *    <br>Ignored when <c>nameFormat</c> is {@link ClassNameFormat#SIMPLE}.
1206    * @param arrayFormat
1207    *    How to format array dimensions.
1208    * @return
1209    *    The formatted class name.
1210    */
1211   public String getNameFormatted(ClassNameFormat nameFormat, boolean includeTypeParams, char separator, ClassArrayFormat arrayFormat) {
1212      var sb = new StringBuilder(128);
1213      appendNameFormatted(sb, nameFormat, includeTypeParams, separator, arrayFormat);
1214      return sb.toString();
1215   }
1216
1217   /**
1218    * Returns the full name of this class.
1219    *
1220    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
1221    * <ul>
1222    *    <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass"</js> - Normal class
1223    *    <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass[][]"</js> - Array.
1224    *    <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass$InnerClass"</js> - Inner class.
1225    *    <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass$InnerClass[][]"</js> - Inner class array.
1226    *    <li><js>"int"</js> - Primitive class.
1227    *    <li><js>"int[][]"</js> - Primitive class class.
1228    *    <li><js>"java.util.Map&lt;java.lang.String,java.lang.Object&gt;"</js> - Parameterized type.
1229    *    <li><js>"java.util.AbstractMap&lt;K,V&gt;"</js> - Parameterized generic type.
1230    *    <li><js>"V"</js> - Parameterized generic type argument.
1231    * </ul>
1232    *
1233    * @return The underlying class name.
1234    */
1235   public String getNameFull() { return nameFull.get(); }
1236
1237   /**
1238    * Same as {@link #getNameSimple()} but uses <js>"Array"</js> instead of <js>"[]"</js>.
1239    *
1240    * @return The readable name for this class.
1241    */
1242   public String getNameReadable() { return nameReadable.get(); }
1243
1244   /**
1245    * Returns all possible names for this class.
1246    *
1247    * @return
1248    *    An array consisting of:
1249    *    <ul>
1250    *       <li>{@link #getNameFull()}
1251    *       <li>{@link Class#getName()} - Note that this might be a dup.
1252    *       <li>{@link #getNameShort()}
1253    *       <li>{@link #getNameSimple()}
1254    *    </ul>
1255    */
1256   public String[] getNames() { return a(getNameFull(), inner.getName(), getNameShort(), getNameSimple()); }
1257
1258   /**
1259    * Returns the short name of the underlying class.
1260    *
1261    * <p>
1262    * Similar to {@link #getNameSimple()} but also renders local or member class name prefixes.
1263    *
1264    * @return The short name of the underlying class.
1265    */
1266   public String getNameShort() { return nameShort.get(); }
1267
1268   /**
1269    * Returns the simple name of the underlying class.
1270    *
1271    * <p>
1272    * Returns either {@link Class#getSimpleName()} or {@link Type#getTypeName()} depending on whether
1273    * this is a class or type.
1274    *
1275    * @return The simple name of the underlying class;
1276    */
1277   public String getNameSimple() { return nn(inner) ? inner.getSimpleName() : innerType.getTypeName(); }
1278
1279   /**
1280    * Returns the nest host of this class.
1281    *
1282    * <p>
1283    * Every class belongs to exactly one nest. A class that is not a member of a nest
1284    * is its own nest host.
1285    *
1286    * @return The nest host of this class.
1287    */
1288   public ClassInfo getNestHost() {
1289      if (inner == null)
1290         return null;
1291      return of(inner.getNestHost());
1292   }
1293
1294   /**
1295    * Returns an array containing all the classes and interfaces that are members of the nest
1296    * to which this class belongs.
1297    *
1298    * @return
1299    *    An unmodifiable list of all classes and interfaces in the same nest as this class.
1300    *    <br>Returns an empty list if this object does not represent a class.
1301    */
1302   public List<ClassInfo> getNestMembers() {
1303      if (inner == null)
1304         return u(l());
1305      var members = inner.getNestMembers();
1306      List<ClassInfo> l = listOfSize(members.length);
1307      for (Class<?> cc : members)
1308         l.add(of(cc));
1309      return u(l);
1310   }
1311
1312   /**
1313    * Locates the no-arg constructor for this class.
1314    *
1315    * <p>
1316    * Constructor must match the visibility requirements specified by parameter 'v'.
1317    * If class is abstract, always returns <jk>null</jk>.
1318    * Note that this also returns the 1-arg constructor for non-static member classes.
1319    *
1320    * @param v The minimum visibility.
1321    * @return The constructor, or <jk>null</jk> if no no-arg constructor exists with the required visibility.
1322    */
1323   public Optional<ConstructorInfo> getNoArgConstructor(Visibility v) {
1324      if (isAbstract())
1325         return oe();
1326      int expectedParams = isNonStaticMemberClass() ? 1 : 0;
1327      // @formatter:off
1328      return getDeclaredConstructors().stream()
1329         .filter(cc -> cc.hasNumParameters(expectedParams))
1330         .filter(cc -> cc.isVisible(v))
1331         .map(cc -> cc.accessible())
1332         .findFirst();
1333      // @formatter:on
1334   }
1335
1336   /**
1337    * Returns the package of this class.
1338    *
1339    * <p>
1340    * Returns <jk>null</jk> in the following cases:
1341    * <ul>
1342    *    <li>Primitive types (e.g., <c>int.class</c>, <c>boolean.class</c>)
1343    *    <li>Array types of primitives (e.g., <c>int[].class</c>)
1344    *    <li>Classes in the default (unnamed) package - while the default package is technically a package,
1345    *       Java's <c>Class.getPackage()</c> returns <jk>null</jk> for classes in the default package because
1346    *       it has no name or associated <c>Package</c> object
1347    * </ul>
1348    *
1349    * @return The package of this class wrapped in a {@link PackageInfo}, or <jk>null</jk> if this class has no package
1350    *    or is in the default (unnamed) package.
1351    */
1352   public PackageInfo getPackage() { return packageInfo.get(); }
1353
1354   /**
1355    * Returns the specified annotation only if it's been declared on the package of this class.
1356    *
1357    * @param <A> The annotation type to look for.
1358    * @param type The annotation class.
1359    * @return The annotation, or <jk>null</jk> if not found.
1360    */
1361   public <A extends Annotation> A getPackageAnnotation(Class<A> type) {
1362      PackageInfo pi = getPackage();
1363      if (pi == null)
1364         return null;
1365      var ai = pi.getAnnotations(type).findFirst().orElse(null);
1366      return ai == null ? null : ai.inner();
1367   }
1368
1369   /**
1370    * Finds the real parameter type of this class.
1371    *
1372    * @param index The zero-based index of the parameter to resolve.
1373    * @param pt The parameterized type class containing the parameterized type to resolve (e.g. <c>HashMap</c>).
1374    * @return The resolved real class.
1375    */
1376   @SuppressWarnings({
1377      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for parameter type resolution
1378   })
1379   public Class<?> getParameterType(int index, Class<?> pt) {
1380      assertArgNotNull(ARG_pt, pt);
1381
1382      // We need to make up a mapping of type names.
1383      var typeMap = new HashMap<Type,Type>();
1384      var cc = inner;
1385      while (pt != cc.getSuperclass()) {
1386         extractTypes(typeMap, cc);
1387         cc = cc.getSuperclass();
1388         assertArg(nn(cc), "Class ''{0}'' is not a subclass of parameterized type ''{1}''", inner.getSimpleName(), pt.getSimpleName());
1389      }
1390
1391      Type gsc = cc.getGenericSuperclass();
1392
1393      assertArg(gsc instanceof ParameterizedType, "Class ''{0}'' is not a parameterized type", pt.getSimpleName());
1394
1395      var cpt = (ParameterizedType)gsc;
1396      Type[] atArgs = cpt.getActualTypeArguments();
1397      assertArg(index < atArgs.length, "Invalid type index. index={0}, argsLength={1}", index, atArgs.length);
1398      Type actualType = cpt.getActualTypeArguments()[index];
1399
1400      if (typeMap.containsKey(actualType))
1401         actualType = typeMap.get(actualType);
1402
1403      if (actualType instanceof Class actualType2) {
1404         return actualType2;
1405
1406      } else if (actualType instanceof GenericArrayType actualType2) {
1407         Type gct = actualType2.getGenericComponentType();
1408         if (gct instanceof ParameterizedType gct2)
1409            return Array.newInstance((Class<?>)gct2.getRawType(), 0).getClass();
1410      } else if (actualType instanceof TypeVariable<?> actualType3) {
1411         for (Class<?> ec = cc.getEnclosingClass(); nn(ec); ec = ec.getEnclosingClass()) {
1412            var outerClass = ec;
1413            var outerTypeMap = new HashMap<Type,Type>();
1414            extractTypes(outerTypeMap, outerClass);
1415            for (var entry : outerTypeMap.entrySet()) {
1416               // HTT - Hard to test - requires nested generics where a type variable in an inner class
1417               // maps to another TypeVariable (not a Class) in the outer class's type map, which then
1418               // needs to be resolved further up the enclosing class chain.
1419               var key = entry.getKey();
1420               var value = entry.getValue();
1421               if (key instanceof TypeVariable<?> key2 && key2.getName().equals(actualType3.getName()) && isInnerClass(key2.getGenericDeclaration(), actualType3.getGenericDeclaration())) {
1422                  if (value instanceof Class<?> value2)
1423                     return value2;
1424                  actualType3 = (TypeVariable<?>)entry.getValue();
1425               }
1426            }
1427         }
1428      } else if (actualType instanceof ParameterizedType actualType2) {
1429         return (Class<?>)actualType2.getRawType();
1430      }
1431      throw iaex("Could not resolve variable ''{0}'' to a type.", actualType.getTypeName());
1432   }
1433
1434   /**
1435    * Extracts generic type parameter mappings from a class's superclass declaration and adds them to a type map.
1436    *
1437    * <p>
1438    * This method builds a mapping between type variables (e.g., <c>T</c>, <c>K</c>, <c>V</c>) and their actual
1439    * type arguments in the inheritance hierarchy. It's used to resolve generic type parameters when navigating
1440    * through class hierarchies.
1441    *
1442    * <p>
1443    * The method handles transitive type mappings by checking if an actual type argument is itself a type variable
1444    * that's already been mapped, and resolving it to its final type.
1445    *
1446    * @param typeMap
1447    *    The map to populate with type variable to actual type mappings.
1448    *    <br>Existing entries are used to resolve transitive mappings.
1449    * @param c
1450    *    The class whose generic superclass should be examined for type parameters.
1451    */
1452   private static void extractTypes(Map<Type,Type> typeMap, Class<?> c) {
1453      var gs = c.getGenericSuperclass();
1454      if (gs instanceof ParameterizedType gs2) {
1455         var typeParameters = ((Class<?>)gs2.getRawType()).getTypeParameters();
1456         var actualTypeArguments = gs2.getActualTypeArguments();
1457         for (var i = 0; i < typeParameters.length; i++) {
1458            if (typeMap.containsKey(actualTypeArguments[i]))
1459               actualTypeArguments[i] = typeMap.get(actualTypeArguments[i]);
1460            typeMap.put(typeParameters[i], actualTypeArguments[i]);
1461         }
1462      }
1463   }
1464
1465   /**
1466    * Checks if one generic declaration (typically a class) is an inner class of another.
1467    *
1468    * <p>
1469    * This method walks up the enclosing class hierarchy of the potential inner class to determine if it's
1470    * nested within the outer class at any level. It's used during generic type resolution to determine if
1471    * a type variable belongs to an inner class that's nested within an outer class.
1472    *
1473    * @param od
1474    *    The potential outer declaration (typically an outer class).
1475    * @param id
1476    *    The potential inner declaration (typically an inner class).
1477    * @return
1478    *    <jk>true</jk> if <c>id</c> is nested within <c>od</c> at any level, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
1479    *    <br>Returns <jk>false</jk> if either parameter is not a <c>Class</c>.
1480    */
1481   private static boolean isInnerClass(GenericDeclaration od, GenericDeclaration id) {
1482      if (od instanceof Class<?> oc && id instanceof Class<?> ic) {
1483         while (nn(ic = ic.getEnclosingClass()))
1484            if (ic == oc)
1485               return true;
1486      }
1487      return false;
1488   }
1489
1490   /**
1491    * Returns a list including this class and all parent classes.
1492    *
1493    * <p>
1494    * Does not include interfaces.
1495    *
1496    * <p>
1497    * Results are in child-to-parent order.
1498    *
1499    * @return An unmodifiable list including this class and all parent classes.
1500    *    <br>Results are in child-to-parent order.
1501    */
1502   public List<ClassInfo> getParents() { return parents.get(); }
1503
1504   /**
1505    * Returns all parent classes and interfaces in proper traversal order.
1506    *
1507    * <p>
1508    * This method returns a unique list of all parent classes (including this class) and all interfaces
1509    * (including interface hierarchies) with proper handling of duplicates. The order is:
1510    * <ol>
1511    *    <li>This class
1512    *    <li>Parent classes in child-to-parent order
1513    *    <li>For each class, interfaces declared on that class and their parent interfaces
1514    * </ol>
1515    *
1516    * <p>
1517    * This is useful for annotation processing where you need to traverse the complete type hierarchy
1518    * without duplicates.
1519    *
1520    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1521    * <p class='bjava'>
1522    *    <jc>// Interface hierarchy:</jc>
1523    *    <jk>interface</jk> ISuperGrandParent {}
1524    *    <jk>interface</jk> IGrandParent <jk>extends</jk> ISuperGrandParent {}
1525    *    <jk>interface</jk> ISuperParent {}
1526    *    <jk>interface</jk> IParent <jk>extends</jk> ISuperParent {}
1527    *    <jk>interface</jk> IChild {}
1528    *
1529    *    <jc>// Class hierarchy:</jc>
1530    *    <jk>class</jk> GrandParent <jk>implements</jk> IGrandParent {}
1531    *    <jk>class</jk> Parent <jk>extends</jk> GrandParent <jk>implements</jk> IParent {}
1532    *    <jk>class</jk> Child <jk>extends</jk> Parent <jk>implements</jk> IChild {}
1533    *
1534    *    <jc>// For Child, returns (in this order):</jc>
1535    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(Child.<jk>class</jk>);
1536    *    List&lt;ClassInfo&gt; <jv>result</jv> = <jv>ci</jv>.getParentsAndInterfaces();
1537    *    <jc>// Result: [</jc>
1538    *    <jc>//   Child,                  // 1. This class</jc>
1539    *    <jc>//   IChild,                 // 2. Interface on Child</jc>
1540    *    <jc>//   Parent,                 // 3. Parent class</jc>
1541    *    <jc>//   IParent,                // 4. Interface on Parent</jc>
1542    *    <jc>//   ISuperParent,           // 5. Parent interface of IParent</jc>
1543    *    <jc>//   GrandParent,            // 6. Grandparent class</jc>
1544    *    <jc>//   IGrandParent,           // 7. Interface on GrandParent</jc>
1545    *    <jc>//   ISuperGrandParent       // 8. Parent interface of IGrandParent</jc>
1546    *    <jc>// ]</jc>
1547    * </p>
1548    *
1549    * @return An unmodifiable list of all parent classes and interfaces, properly ordered without duplicates.
1550    */
1551   public List<ClassInfo> getParentsAndInterfaces() { return parentsAndInterfaces.get(); }
1552
1553   /**
1554    * Returns the permitted subclasses of this sealed class.
1555    *
1556    * <p>
1557    * If this class is not sealed, returns an empty list.
1558    *
1559    * @return
1560    *    An unmodifiable list of permitted subclasses if this is a sealed class.
1561    *    <br>Returns an empty list if this class is not sealed.
1562    */
1563   public List<ClassInfo> getPermittedSubclasses() {
1564      if (inner == null || ! inner.isSealed())
1565         return u(l());
1566      return u(stream(inner.getPermittedSubclasses()).map(ClassInfo::of).toList());
1567   }
1568
1569   /**
1570    * Returns the default value for this primitive class.
1571    *
1572    * @return The default value, or <jk>null</jk> if this is not a primitive class.
1573    */
1574   public Object getPrimitiveDefault() { return primitiveDefaultMap.get(inner); }
1575
1576   /**
1577    * If this class is a primitive wrapper (e.g. <code><jk>Integer</jk>.<jk>class</jk></code>) returns it's
1578    * primitive class (e.g. <code>int.<jk>class</jk></code>).
1579    *
1580    * @return The primitive class, or <jk>null</jk> if class is not a primitive wrapper.
1581    */
1582   public Class<?> getPrimitiveForWrapper() { return pmap2.get(inner); }
1583
1584   /**
1585    * If this class is a primitive (e.g. <code><jk>int</jk>.<jk>class</jk></code>) returns it's wrapper class
1586    * (e.g. <code>Integer.<jk>class</jk></code>).
1587    *
1588    * @return The wrapper class, or <jk>null</jk> if class is not a primitive.
1589    */
1590   public Class<?> getPrimitiveWrapper() { return pmap1.get(inner); }
1591
1592   /**
1593    * Returns the {@link ProtectionDomain} of this class.
1594    *
1595    * <p>
1596    * If a security manager is installed, this method requires <c>RuntimePermission("getProtectionDomain")</c>.
1597    *
1598    * @return The {@link ProtectionDomain} of this class, or <jk>null</jk> if the class does not have a protection domain.
1599    */
1600   public ProtectionDomain getProtectionDomain() { return inner == null ? null : inner.getProtectionDomain(); }
1601
1602   /**
1603    * Returns the first matching public constructor on this class.
1604    *
1605    * @param filter A predicate to apply to the entries to determine if value should be used.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1606    * @return The public constructor that matches the specified predicate.
1607    */
1608   public Optional<ConstructorInfo> getPublicConstructor(Predicate<ConstructorInfo> filter) {
1609      return publicConstructors.get().stream().filter(x -> t(filter, x)).findFirst();
1610   }
1611
1612   /**
1613    * Returns all the public constructors defined on this class.
1614    *
1615    * @return All public constructors defined on this class.
1616    */
1617   public List<ConstructorInfo> getPublicConstructors() { return publicConstructors.get(); }
1618
1619   /**
1620    * Returns the first matching public field on this class.
1621    *
1622    * @param filter A predicate to apply to the entries to determine if value should be used.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1623    * @return The public field, or <jk>null</jk> if not found.
1624    */
1625   public Optional<FieldInfo> getPublicField(Predicate<FieldInfo> filter) {
1626      return publicFields.get().stream().filter(x -> t(filter, x)).findFirst();
1627   }
1628
1629   /**
1630    * Returns all public fields on this class.
1631    *
1632    * <p>
1633    * Hidden fields are excluded from the results.
1634    *
1635    * @return
1636    *    All public fields on this class.
1637    *    <br>Results are in alphabetical order.
1638    *    <br>List is unmodifiable.
1639    */
1640   public List<FieldInfo> getPublicFields() { return publicFields.get(); }
1641
1642   /**
1643    * Returns the first matching public method on this class.
1644    *
1645    * @param filter A predicate to apply to the entries to determine if value should be used.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1646    * @return The first matching method, or <jk>null</jk> if no methods matched.
1647    */
1648   public Optional<MethodInfo> getPublicMethod(Predicate<MethodInfo> filter) {
1649      return publicMethods.get().stream().filter(x -> t(filter, x)).findFirst();
1650   }
1651
1652   /**
1653    * Finds a public getter method for the specified bean property name.
1654    *
1655    * <p>
1656    * Matches any public no-argument method named {@code get<PropertyName>} with a non-void return type,
1657    * or {@code is<PropertyName>} with a {@code boolean}/{@link Boolean} return type.
1658    *
1659    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1660    * <p class='bjava'>
1661    *    <jc>// Find the getter for the "name" property on MyBean</jc>
1662    *    Optional&lt;MethodInfo&gt; <jv>getter</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>info</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>).findGetter(<js>"name"</js>);
1663    *    <jv>getter</jv>.ifPresent(<jv>m</jv> -&gt; System.<jsf>out</jsf>.println(<jv>m</jv>.getName()));  <jc>// "getName"</jc>
1664    * </p>
1665    *
1666    * @param propertyName The bean property name (e.g. <js>"name"</js> to match {@code getName()} or {@code isName()}).
1667    * @return The matching getter method, or an empty {@link Optional} if not found.
1668    */
1669   public Optional<MethodInfo> findGetter(String propertyName) {
1670      return getPublicMethod(m -> m.isGetter() && m.getPropertyName().equals(propertyName));
1671   }
1672
1673   /**
1674    * Finds a public setter method for the specified bean property name.
1675    *
1676    * <p>
1677    * Matches any public single-argument method named {@code set<PropertyName>}.
1678    *
1679    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1680    * <p class='bjava'>
