1681    *    <jc>// Find the setter for the "name" property on MyBean</jc>
1682    *    Optional&lt;MethodInfo&gt; <jv>setter</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>info</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>).findSetter(<js>"name"</js>);
1683    *    <jv>setter</jv>.ifPresent(<jv>m</jv> -&gt; System.<jsf>out</jsf>.println(<jv>m</jv>.getName()));  <jc>// "setName"</jc>
1684    * </p>
1685    *
1686    * @param propertyName The bean property name (e.g. <js>"name"</js> to match {@code setName(X)}).
1687    * @return The matching setter method, or an empty {@link Optional} if not found.
1688    */
1689   public Optional<MethodInfo> findSetter(String propertyName) {
1690      return getPublicMethod(m -> m.isSetter() && m.getPropertyName().equals(propertyName));
1691   }
1692
1693   /**
1694    * Returns all public methods on this class and parent classes.
1695    *
1696    * <p>
1697    * This method returns <b>public methods only</b>, from this class and all parent classes and interfaces.
1698    *
1699    * <h5 class='section'>Comparison with Similar Methods:</h5>
1700    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
1701    *    <li>{@link #getDeclaredMethods()} - Returns all declared methods on this class only (all visibility levels)
1702    *    <li>{@link #getAllMethods()} - Returns all declared methods on this class and parents (all visibility levels)
1703    *    <li>{@link #getPublicMethods()} - Returns public methods only on this class and parents ← This method
1704    * </ul>
1705    *
1706    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5>
1707    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
1708    *    <li>This method behaves similarly to Java's {@link Class#getMethods()}, returning only public methods from this class and parent classes/interfaces.
1709    *    <li>Bridge and synthetic methods are included (matching {@link Class#getMethods()} behavior).
1710    *    <li>Methods defined on the {@link Object} class are excluded from the results.
1711    * </ul>
1712    *
1713    * @return
1714    *    All public methods on this class and parent classes.
1715    *    <br>Results are ordered alphabetically.
1716    *    <br>List is unmodifiable.
1717    */
1718   public List<MethodInfo> getPublicMethods() { return publicMethods.get(); }
1719
1720   /**
1721    * Returns the record components of this record class.
1722    *
1723    * <p>
1724    * Returns a cached, unmodifiable list of record components.
1725    * The components are returned in the same order as they appear in the record declaration.
1726    * If this class is not a record, returns an empty list.
1727    *
1728    * @return An unmodifiable list of record components, or an empty list if this class is not a record.
1729    */
1730   public List<RecordComponent> getRecordComponents() { return recordComponents.get(); }
1731
1732   /**
1733    * Returns the repeated annotation method on this class.
1734    *
1735    * <p>
1736    * The repeated annotation method is the <code>value()</code> method that returns an array
1737    * of annotations who themselves are marked with the {@link Repeatable @Repeatable} annotation
1738    * of this class.
1739    *
1740    * @return The repeated annotation method on this class, or <jk>null</jk> if it doesn't exist.
1741    */
1742   public MethodInfo getRepeatedAnnotationMethod() { return repeatedAnnotationMethod.get(); }
1743
1744   /**
1745    * Finds a resource with a given name.
1746    *
1747    * <p>
1748    * The rules for searching resources associated with a given class are implemented by the defining class loader.
1749    *
1750    * @param name The resource name.
1751    * @return A URL object for reading the resource, or <jk>null</jk> if the resource could not be found.
1752    */
1753   public java.net.URL getResource(String name) {
1754      return inner == null ? null : inner.getResource(name);
1755   }
1756
1757   /**
1758    * Finds a resource with a given name and returns an input stream for reading the resource.
1759    *
1760    * <p>
1761    * The rules for searching resources associated with a given class are implemented by the defining class loader.
1762    *
1763    * @param name The resource name.
1764    * @return An input stream for reading the resource, or <jk>null</jk> if the resource could not be found.
1765    */
1766   @SuppressWarnings({
1767      "resource" // Caller takes ownership of the returned InputStream
1768   })
1769   public java.io.InputStream getResourceAsStream(String name) {
1770      return inner == null ? null : inner.getResourceAsStream(name);
1771   }
1772
1773   /**
1774    * Returns the signers of this class.
1775    *
1776    * <p>
1777    * Returns a cached, unmodifiable list.
1778    *
1779    * @return An unmodifiable list of signers, or an empty list if there are no signers.
1780    */
1781   public List<Object> getSigners() { return signers.get(); }
1782
1783   /**
1784    * Returns the parent class.
1785    *
1786    * @return
1787    *    The parent class, or <jk>null</jk> if the class has no parent.
1788    */
1789   public ClassInfo getSuperclass() {
1790      if (inner == null)
1791         return null;
1792      var sc = inner.getSuperclass();
1793      return sc == null ? null : of(sc);
1794   }
1795
1796   /**
1797    * Returns a list of {@link TypeVariable} objects that represent the type variables declared by this class.
1798    *
1799    * <p>
1800    * Returns a cached, unmodifiable list.
1801    * The type variables are returned in the same order as they appear in the class declaration.
1802    *
1803    * @return
1804    *    An unmodifiable list of {@link TypeVariable} objects representing the type parameters of this class.
1805    *    <br>Returns an empty list if this class declares no type parameters.
1806    */
1807   public List<TypeVariable<?>> getTypeParameters() { return typeParameters.get(); }
1808
1809   /**
1810    * Returns the wrapper class if this is a primitive, otherwise returns this class.
1811    *
1812    * <p>
1813    * If this class is a primitive (e.g. {@code int.class}), returns its wrapper class
1814    * (e.g. {@code Integer.class}) wrapped in a {@link ClassInfo}.
1815    * Otherwise, returns this {@link ClassInfo} instance.
1816    *
1817    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1818    * <p class='bjava'>
1819    *    ClassInfo <jv>intClass</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jk>int</jk>.<jk>class</jk>);
1820    *    ClassInfo <jv>wrapper</jv> = <jv>intClass</jv>.getWrapperIfPrimitive();  <jc>// Returns ClassInfo for Integer.class</jc>
1821    *
1822    *    ClassInfo <jv>stringClass</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>);
1823    *    ClassInfo <jv>same</jv> = <jv>stringClass</jv>.getWrapperIfPrimitive();  <jc>// Returns same ClassInfo</jc>
1824    * </p>
1825    *
1826    * @return The wrapper {@link ClassInfo} if this is a primitive, or this {@link ClassInfo} if not a primitive.
1827    */
1828   public ClassInfo getWrapperIfPrimitive() {
1829      if (inner == null || ! inner.isPrimitive())
1830         return this;
1831      return of(pmap1.get(inner));
1832   }
1833
1834   /**
1835    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class has the specified annotation.
1836    *
1837    * @param <A> The annotation type to look for.
1838    * @param type The annotation to look for.
1839    * @return The <jk>true</jk> if annotation if found.
1840    */
1841   public <A extends Annotation> boolean hasAnnotation(Class<A> type) {
1842      return getAnnotations(type).findFirst().isPresent();
1843   }
1844
1845   @Override
1846   public int hashCode() {
1847      return innerType.hashCode();
1848   }
1849
1850   /**
1851    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is not in the root package.
1852    *
1853    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is not in the root package.
1854    */
1855   public boolean hasPackage() {
1856      return nn(getPackage());
1857   }
1858
1859   /**
1860    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the {@link #getPrimitiveWrapper()} method returns a value.
1861    *
1862    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the {@link #getPrimitiveWrapper()} method returns a value.
1863    */
1864   public boolean hasPrimitiveWrapper() {
1865      return pmap1.containsKey(inner);
1866   }
1867
1868   /**
1869    * Returns the wrapped class as a {@link Class}.
1870    *
1871    * @param <T> The inner class type.
1872    * @return The wrapped class as a {@link Class}, or <jk>null</jk> if it's not a class (e.g. it's a {@link ParameterizedType}).
1873    */
1874   public <T> Class<T> inner() {
1875      return (Class<T>)inner;
1876   }
1877
1878   /**
1879    * Returns the wrapped class as a {@link Type}.
1880    *
1881    * @return The wrapped class as a {@link Type}.
1882    */
1883   public Type innerType() {
1884      return innerType;
1885   }
1886
1887   /**
1888    * Checks for equality with the specified class.
1889    *
1890    * @param c The class to check equality with.
1891    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified class is the same as this one.
1892    */
1893   public boolean is(Class<?> c) {
1894      return nn(this.inner) && this.inner.equals(c);
1895   }
1896
1897   /**
1898    * Checks for equality with the specified class.
1899    *
1900    * @param c The class to check equality with.
1901    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified class is the same as this one.
1902    */
1903   public boolean is(ClassInfo c) {
1904      if (nn(this.inner))
1905         return this.inner.equals(c.inner());
1906      return innerType.equals(c.innerType);
1907   }
1908
1909   /**
1910    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if all specified flags are applicable to this class.
1911    *
1912    * @param flag The flag to test for.
1913    * @return <jk>true</jk> if all specified flags are applicable to this class.
1914    */
1915   @Override
1916   public boolean is(ElementFlag flag) {
1917      return switch (flag) {
1918         case ANNOTATION -> isAnnotation();
1919         case NOT_ANNOTATION -> ! isAnnotation();
1920         case ANONYMOUS -> isAnonymousClass();
1921         case NOT_ANONYMOUS -> ! isAnonymousClass();
1922         case ARRAY -> isArray();
1923         case NOT_ARRAY -> ! isArray();
1924         case CLASS -> ! isInterface();
1925         case DEPRECATED -> isDeprecated();
1926         case NOT_DEPRECATED -> isNotDeprecated();
1927         case ENUM -> isEnum();
1928         case NOT_ENUM -> ! isEnum();
1929         case LOCAL -> isLocalClass();
1930         case NOT_LOCAL -> ! isLocalClass();
1931         case MEMBER -> isMemberClass();
1932         case NOT_MEMBER -> isNotMemberClass();
1933         case NON_STATIC_MEMBER -> isNonStaticMemberClass();
1934         case NOT_NON_STATIC_MEMBER -> ! isNonStaticMemberClass();
1935         case PRIMITIVE -> isPrimitive();
1936         case NOT_PRIMITIVE -> ! isPrimitive();
1937         case RECORD -> isRecord();
1938         case NOT_RECORD -> ! isRecord();
1939         case SEALED -> isSealed();
1940         case NOT_SEALED -> ! isSealed();
1941         case SYNTHETIC -> isSynthetic();
1942         case NOT_SYNTHETIC -> ! isSynthetic(); // HTT - synthetic classes are compiler-generated and difficult to create in tests
1943         default -> super.is(flag);
1944      };
1945   }
1946
1947   /**
1948    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is an annotation.
1949    *
1950    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is an annotation.
1951    */
1952   public boolean isAnnotation() { return nn(inner) && inner.isAnnotation(); }
1953
1954   /**
1955    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is an anonymous class.
1956    *
1957    * <p>
1958    * An anonymous class is a local class declared within a method or constructor that has no name.
1959    *
1960    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is an anonymous class.
1961    */
1962   public boolean isAnonymousClass() { return nn(inner) && inner.isAnonymousClass(); }
1963
1964   /**
1965    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is any of the specified types.
1966    *
1967    * @param types The types to check against.
1968    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is any of the specified types.
1969    */
1970   public boolean isAny(Class<?>...types) {
1971      for (var cc : types)
1972         if (is(cc))
1973            return true;
1974      return false;
1975   }
1976
1977   /**
1978    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is any of the specified types.
1979    *
1980    * <p>
1981    * This is a high-performance overload that avoids array creation and iteration.
1982    * Use this method instead of {@link #isAny(Class...)} when checking against exactly 2 types.
1983    *
1984    * @param t1 The first type to check against.
1985    * @param t2 The second type to check against.
1986    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is any of the specified types.
1987    */
1988   public boolean isAny(Class<?> t1, Class<?> t2) {
1989      return inner == t1 || inner == t2;
1990   }
1991
1992   /**
1993    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is any of the specified types.
1994    *
1995    * <p>
1996    * This is a high-performance overload that avoids array creation and iteration.
1997    * Use this method instead of {@link #isAny(Class...)} when checking against exactly 3 types.
1998    *
1999    * @param t1 The first type to check against.
2000    * @param t2 The second type to check against.
2001    * @param t3 The third type to check against.
2002    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is any of the specified types.
2003    */
2004   public boolean isAny(Class<?> t1, Class<?> t2, Class<?> t3) {
2005      return inner == t1 || inner == t2 || inner == t3;
2006   }
2007
2008   /**
2009    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is any of the specified types.
2010    *
2011    * <p>
2012    * This is a high-performance overload that avoids array creation and iteration.
2013    * Use this method instead of {@link #isAny(Class...)} when checking against exactly 4 types.
2014    *
2015    * @param t1 The first type to check against.
2016    * @param t2 The second type to check against.
2017    * @param t3 The third type to check against.
2018    * @param t4 The fourth type to check against.
2019    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is any of the specified types.
2020    */
2021   public boolean isAny(Class<?> t1, Class<?> t2, Class<?> t3, Class<?> t4) {
2022      return inner == t1 || inner == t2 || inner == t3 || inner == t4;
2023   }
2024
2025   /**
2026    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is any of the specified types.
2027    *
2028    * <p>
2029    * This is a high-performance overload that avoids array creation and iteration.
2030    * Use this method instead of {@link #isAny(Class...)} when checking against exactly 5 types.
2031    *
2032    * @param t1 The first type to check against.
2033    * @param t2 The second type to check against.
2034    * @param t3 The third type to check against.
2035    * @param t4 The fourth type to check against.
2036    * @param t5 The fifth type to check against.
2037    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is any of the specified types.
2038    */
2039   public boolean isAny(Class<?> t1, Class<?> t2, Class<?> t3, Class<?> t4, Class<?> t5) {
2040      return inner == t1 || inner == t2 || inner == t3 || inner == t4 || inner == t5;
2041   }
2042
2043   /**
2044    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is an array.
2045    *
2046    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is an array.
2047    */
2048   public boolean isArray() { return nn(inner) && inner.isArray(); }
2049
2050   /**
2051    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a child or the same as <c>parent</c>.
2052    *
2053    * @param parent The parent class.
2054    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a child or the same as <c>parent</c>.
2055    */
2056   public boolean isAssignableTo(Class<?> parent) {
2057      return and(nn(inner), nn(parent)) && parent.isAssignableFrom(inner);
2058   }
2059
2060   /**
2061    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a child of <c>parent</c> (not the same class).
2062    *
2063    * <p>
2064    * This method returns <jk>true</jk> only if this class is a child of the parent class,
2065    * excluding the case where they are the same class.
2066    *
2067    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2068    * <p class='bjava'>
2069    *    ClassInfo <jv>child</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(ArrayList.<jk>class</jk>);
2070    *    ClassInfo <jv>parent</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(List.<jk>class</jk>);
2071    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>b1</jv> = <jv>child</jv>.isChildOf(List.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// true</jc>
2072    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>b2</jv> = <jv>child</jv>.isChildOf(ArrayList.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// false (same class)</jc>
2073    * </p>
2074    *
2075    * @param parent The parent class.
2076    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a child of <c>parent</c>.
2077    */
2078   public boolean isChildOf(Class<?> parent) {
2079      return and(nn(inner), nn(parent)) && neq(inner, parent) && parent.isAssignableFrom(inner);
2080   }
2081
2082   /**
2083    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a child of <c>parent</c> (not the same class).
2084    *
2085    * <p>
2086    * Same as {@link #isChildOf(Class)} but takes in a {@link ClassInfo}.
2087    *
2088    * @param parent The parent class.
2089    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a child of <c>parent</c>.
2090    */
2091   public boolean isChildOf(ClassInfo parent) {
2092      return and(nn(inner), nn(parent)) && neq(inner, parent.inner()) && parent.inner().isAssignableFrom(inner);
2093   }
2094
2095   /**
2096    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a child of <c>parent</c> (not the same class).
2097    *
2098    * @param parent The parent class.
2099    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a child of <c>parent</c>.
2100    */
2101   public boolean isChildOf(Type parent) {
2102      if (parent instanceof Class<?> c)
2103         return isChildOf(c);
2104      return false;
2105   }
2106
2107   /**
2108    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a child or the same as <c>parent</c>.
2109    *
2110    * @param parent The parent class.
2111    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>parent</c>.
2112    */
2113   public boolean isAssignableTo(ClassInfo parent) {
2114      return isAssignableTo(parent.inner());
2115   }
2116
2117   /**
2118    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a child or the same as <c>parent</c>.
2119    *
2120    * @param parent The parent class.
2121    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>parent</c>.
2122    */
2123   public boolean isAssignableTo(Type parent) {
2124      if (parent instanceof Class<?> c)
2125         return isAssignableTo(c);
2126      return false;
2127   }
2128
2129   /**
2130    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a child or the same as any of the <c>parents</c>.
2131    *
2132    * @param parents The parents class.
2133    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a child or the same as any of the <c>parents</c>.
2134    */
2135   public boolean isAssignableToAny(Class<?>...parents) {
2136      for (var p : parents)
2137         if (isAssignableTo(p))
2138            return true;
2139      return false;
2140   }
2141
2142   /**
2143    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is <jk>boolean</jk> or {@link Boolean}.
2144    *
2145    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is <jk>boolean</jk> or {@link Boolean}.
2146    */
2147   public boolean isBoolean() { return isAny(boolean.class, Boolean.class); }
2148
2149   /**
2150    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is assignable to {@link CharSequence}.
2151    *
2152    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is assignable to {@link CharSequence}.
2153    */
2154   public boolean isCharSequence() { return isAssignableTo(CharSequence.class); }
2155
2156   /**
2157    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is not an interface.
2158    *
2159    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is not an interface.
2160    */
2161   public boolean isClass() { return nn(inner) && ! inner.isInterface(); }
2162
2163   /**
2164    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is assignable to {@link Collection}.
2165    *
2166    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is assignable to {@link Collection}.
2167    */
2168   public boolean isCollection() { return isAssignableTo(Collection.class); }
2169
2170   /**
2171    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Collection} or an array.
2172    *
2173    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Collection} or an array.
2174    */
2175   public boolean isCollectionOrArray() { return nn(inner) && (Collection.class.isAssignableFrom(inner) || inner.isArray()); }
2176
2177   /**
2178    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is assignable to {@link Date} or {@link Calendar}.
2179    *
2180    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is assignable to {@link Date} or {@link Calendar}.
2181    */
2182   public boolean isDateOrCalendar() { return isAssignableToAny(Date.class, Calendar.class); }
2183
2184   /**
2185    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a decimal number type ({@link Float}, {@link Double}, <jk>float</jk>, or <jk>double</jk>).
2186    *
2187    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a decimal number type.
2188    */
2189   public boolean isDecimal() { return isAssignableToAny(Float.class, Double.class) || isAny(float.class, double.class); }
2190
2191   /**
2192    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class has the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
2193    *
2194    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class has the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
2195    */
2196   public boolean isDeprecated() { return nn(inner) && inner.isAnnotationPresent(Deprecated.class); }
2197
2198   /**
2199    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is {@link Double} or <jk>double</jk>.
2200    *
2201    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is {@link Double} or <jk>double</jk>.
2202    */
2203   public boolean isDouble() { return isAny(Double.class, double.class); }
2204
2205   /**
2206    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is an enum.
2207    *
2208    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is an enum.
2209    */
2210   public boolean isEnum() { return nn(inner) && inner.isEnum(); }
2211
2212   /**
2213    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is {@link Long} or <jk>long</jk>.
2214    *
2215    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is {@link Long} or <jk>long</jk>.
2216    */
2217   public boolean isLong() { return isAny(Long.class, long.class); }
2218
2219   /**
2220    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is assignable to {@link Map}.
2221    *
2222    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is assignable to {@link Map}.
2223    */
2224   public boolean isMap() { return isAssignableTo(Map.class); }
2225
2226   /**
2227    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a numeric type (assignable to {@link Number} or a numeric primitive).
2228    *
2229    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a numeric type.
2230    */
2231   public boolean isNumber() { return isAssignableTo(Number.class) || isAny(byte.class, short.class, int.class, long.class, float.class, double.class); }
2232
2233   /**
2234    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is exactly {@link Object}.
2235    *
2236    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is {@link Object}.
2237    */
2238   public boolean isObject() { return is(Object.class); }
2239
2240   /**
2241    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is {@link Optional}.
2242    *
2243    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is {@link Optional}.
2244    */
2245   public boolean isOptional() { return is(Optional.class); }
2246
2247   /**
2248    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is assignable to {@link java.time.temporal.Temporal}.
2249    *
2250    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is assignable to {@link java.time.temporal.Temporal}.
2251    */
2252   public boolean isTemporal() { return isAssignableTo(java.time.temporal.Temporal.class); }
2253
2254   /**
2255    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified value is an instance of this class.
2256    *
2257    * @param value The value to check.
2258    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified value is an instance of this class.
2259    */
2260   public boolean isInstance(Object value) {
2261      if (nn(this.inner))
2262         return inner.isInstance(value);
2263      return false;
2264   }
2265
2266   /**
2267    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a local class.
2268    *
2269    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a local class.
2270    */
2271   public boolean isLocalClass() { return nn(inner) && inner.isLocalClass(); }
2272
2273   /**
2274    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a member class.
2275    *
2276    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a member class.
2277    */
2278   public boolean isMemberClass() { return nn(inner) && inner.isMemberClass(); }
2279
2280   /**
2281    * Determines if this class and the specified class are nestmates.
2282    *
2283    * <p>
2284    * Two classes are nestmates if they belong to the same nest.
2285    *
2286    * @param c The class to check.
2287    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class and the specified class are nestmates.
2288    */
2289   public boolean isNestmateOf(Class<?> c) {
2290      return nn(this.inner) && nn(c) && this.inner.isNestmateOf(c);
2291   }
2292
2293   /**
2294    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a member class and not static.
2295    *
2296    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a member class and not static.
2297    */
2298   public boolean isNonStaticMemberClass() { return nn(inner) && inner.isMemberClass() && ! isStatic(); }
2299
2300   /**
2301    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class doesn't have the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
2302    *
2303    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class doesn't have the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
2304    */
2305   public boolean isNotDeprecated() { return inner == null || ! inner.isAnnotationPresent(Deprecated.class); }
2306
2307   /**
2308    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a local class.
2309    *
2310    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a local class.
2311    */
2312   public boolean isNotLocalClass() { return inner == null || ! inner.isLocalClass(); }
2313
2314   /**
2315    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a member class.
2316    *
2317    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a member class.
2318    */
2319   public boolean isNotMemberClass() { return inner == null || ! inner.isMemberClass(); }
2320
2321   /**
2322    * Returns <jk>false</jk> if this class is a member class and not static.
2323    *
2324    * @return <jk>false</jk> if this class is a member class and not static.
2325    */
2326   public boolean isNotNonStaticMemberClass() { return ! isNonStaticMemberClass(); }
2327
2328   /**
2329    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this is not a primitive class.
2330    *
2331    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this is not a primitive class.
2332    */
2333   public boolean isNotPrimitive() { return inner == null || ! inner.isPrimitive(); }
2334
2335   /**
2336    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is <c><jk>void</jk>.<jk>class</jk></c> or {@link Void} or has the simple name <js>"Void</js>.
2337    *
2338    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is <c><jk>void</jk>.<jk>class</jk></c> or {@link Void} or has the simple name <js>"Void</js>.
2339    */
2340   public boolean isVoid() {
2341      return inner != null && (inner == void.class || inner == Void.class || inner.getSimpleName().equalsIgnoreCase("void"));
2342   }
2343
2344   /**
2345    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is not <c><jk>void</jk>.<jk>class</jk></c> and not {@link Void} and does not have the simple name <js>"Void</js>.
2346    *
2347    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is not <c><jk>void</jk>.<jk>class</jk></c> and not {@link Void} and does not have the simple name <js>"Void</js>.
2348    */
2349   public boolean isNotVoid() { return ! isVoid(); }
2350
2351   /**
2352    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2353    *
2354    * @param child The child class.
2355    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2356    */
2357   public boolean isAssignableFrom(Class<?> child) {
2358      return nn(inner) && nn(child) && inner.isAssignableFrom(child);
2359   }
2360
2361   /**
2362    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a parent of <c>child</c> (not the same class).
2363    *
2364    * <p>
2365    * This method returns <jk>true</jk> only if this class is a parent of the child class,
2366    * excluding the case where they are the same class.
2367    *
2368    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2369    * <p class='bjava'>
2370    *    ClassInfo <jv>parent</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(List.<jk>class</jk>);
2371    *    ClassInfo <jv>child</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(ArrayList.<jk>class</jk>);
2372    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>b1</jv> = <jv>parent</jv>.isParentOf(ArrayList.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// true</jc>
2373    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>b2</jv> = <jv>parent</jv>.isParentOf(List.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// false (same class)</jc>
2374    * </p>
2375    *
2376    * @param child The child class.
2377    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a parent of <c>child</c>.
2378    */
2379   public boolean isParentOf(Class<?> child) {
2380      return nn(inner) && nn(child) && neq(inner, child) && inner.isAssignableFrom(child);
2381   }
2382
2383   /**
2384    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a parent of <c>child</c> (not the same class).
2385    *
2386    * <p>
2387    * Same as {@link #isParentOf(Class)} but takes in a {@link ClassInfo}.
2388    *
2389    * @param child The child class.
2390    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a parent of <c>child</c>.
2391    */
2392   public boolean isParentOf(ClassInfo child) {
2393      return nn(inner) && nn(child) && neq(inner, child.inner()) && inner.isAssignableFrom(child.inner());
2394   }
2395
2396   /**
2397    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a parent of <c>child</c> (not the same class).
2398    *
2399    * @param child The child class.
2400    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is strictly a parent of <c>child</c>.
2401    */
2402   public boolean isParentOf(Type child) {
2403      if (child instanceof Class<?> c)
2404         return isParentOf(c);
2405      return false;
2406   }
2407
2408   /**
2409    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2410    *
2411    * <p>
2412    * Same as {@link #isAssignableFrom(Class)} but takes in a {@link ClassInfo}.
2413    *
2414    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2415    * <p class='bjava'>
2416    *    ClassInfo <jv>parent</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(List.<jk>class</jk>);
2417    *    ClassInfo <jv>child</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(ArrayList.<jk>class</jk>);
2418    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>b</jv> = <jv>parent</jv>.isParentOf(<jv>child</jv>);  <jc>// true</jc>
2419    * </p>
2420    *
2421    * @param child The child class.
2422    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2423    */
2424   public boolean isAssignableFrom(ClassInfo child) {
2425      return nn(inner) && nn(child) && inner.isAssignableFrom(child.inner());
2426   }
2427
2428   /**
2429    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2430    *
2431    * @param child The child class.
2432    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2433    */
2434   public boolean isAssignableFrom(Type child) {
2435      if (child instanceof Class<?> c)
2436         return isAssignableFrom(c);
2437      return false;
2438   }
2439
2440   /**
2441    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2442    *
2443    * <p>
2444    * Primitive classes are converted to wrapper classes and compared.
2445    *
2446    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
2447    * <p class='bjava'>
2448    *       ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>).isParentOfLenient(String.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// true</jc>
2449    *       ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(CharSequence.<jk>class</jk>).isParentOfLenient(String.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// true</jc>
2450    *       ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>).isParentOfLenient(CharSequence.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// false</jc>
2451    *       ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jk>int</jk>.<jk>class</jk>).isParentOfLenient(Integer.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// true</jc>
2452    *       ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(Integer.<jk>class</jk>).isParentOfLenient(<jk>int</jk>.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// true</jc>
2453    *       ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(Number.<jk>class</jk>).isParentOfLenient(<jk>int</jk>.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// true</jc>
2454    *       ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jk>int</jk>.<jk>class</jk>).isParentOfLenient(Number.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// false</jc>
2455    *       ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jk>int</jk>.<jk>class</jk>).isParentOfLenient(<jk>long</jk>.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// false</jc>
2456    * </p>
2457    *
2458    * @param child The child class.
2459    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2460    */
2461   public boolean isParentOfLenient(Class<?> child) {
2462      if (inner == null || child == null)
2463         return false;
2464      if (inner.isAssignableFrom(child))
2465         return true;
2466      if (this.isPrimitive() || child.isPrimitive()) {
2467         return this.getWrapperIfPrimitive().isAssignableFrom(of(child).getWrapperIfPrimitive());
2468      }
2469      return false;
2470   }
2471
2472   /**
2473    * Same as {@link #isParentOfLenient(Class)} but takes in a {@link ClassInfo}.
2474    *
2475    * @param child The child class.
2476    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2477    */
2478   public boolean isParentOfLenient(ClassInfo child) {
2479      if (inner == null || child == null)
2480         return false;
2481      if (inner.isAssignableFrom(child.inner()))
2482         return true;
2483      // If at least one is a primitive, convert both to wrappers and compare
2484      // Note: If both are primitives and the same type, we would have returned true above
2485      // So this branch is only reached when at least one is a primitive and they're not directly assignable
2486      if (this.isPrimitive() || child.isPrimitive()) {
2487         return this.getWrapperIfPrimitive().isAssignableFrom(child.getWrapperIfPrimitive());
2488      }
2489      return false;
2490   }
2491
2492   /**
2493    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2494    *
2495    * @param child The child class.
2496    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent or the same as <c>child</c>.
2497    */
2498   public boolean isParentOfLenient(Type child) {
2499      if (child instanceof Class<?> c)
2500         return isParentOfLenient(c);
2501      return false;
2502   }
2503
2504   /**
2505    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this is a primitive class.
2506    *
2507    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this is a primitive class.
2508    */
2509   public boolean isPrimitive() { return nn(inner) && inner.isPrimitive(); }
2510
2511   /**
2512    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a record class.
2513    *
2514    * <p>
2515    * A record class is a final class that extends {@link Record}.
2516    *
2517    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a record class.
2518    */
2519   public boolean isRecord() { return nn(inner) && inner.isRecord(); }
2520
2521   /**
2522    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this is a repeated annotation class.
2523    *
2524    * <p>
2525    * A repeated annotation has a single <code>value()</code> method that returns an array
2526    * of annotations who themselves are marked with the {@link Repeatable @Repeatable} annotation
2527    * of this class.
2528    *
2529    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this is a repeated annotation class.
2530    */
2531   public boolean isRepeatedAnnotation() { return getRepeatedAnnotationMethod() != null; }
2532
2533   /**
2534    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link RuntimeException}.
2535    *
2536    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link RuntimeException}.
2537    */
2538   public boolean isRuntimeException() { return isAssignableTo(RuntimeException.class); }
2539
2540   /**
2541    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a sealed class.
2542    *
2543    * <p>
2544    * A sealed class is a class that can only be extended by a permitted set of subclasses.
2545    *
2546    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a sealed class.
2547    */
2548   public boolean isSealed() { return nn(inner) && inner.isSealed(); }
2549
2550   /**
2551    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a child of <c>parent</c>.
2552    *
2553    * @param parent The parent class.
2554    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a parent of <c>child</c>.
2555    */
2556   public boolean isStrictChildOf(Class<?> parent) {
2557      return nn(inner) && nn(parent) && parent.isAssignableFrom(inner) && ! inner.equals(parent);
2558   }
2559
2560   /**
2561    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a synthetic class.
2562    *
2563    * <p>
2564    * A synthetic class is one that is generated by the compiler and does not appear in source code.
2565    *
2566    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is synthetic.
2567    */
2568   public boolean isSynthetic() { return nn(inner) && inner.isSynthetic(); }  // HTT
2569
2570   /**
2571    * Identifies if the specified visibility matches this constructor.
2572    *
2573    * @param v The visibility to validate against.
2574    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this visibility matches the modifier attribute of this constructor.
2575    */
2576   public boolean isVisible(Visibility v) {
2577      return nn(inner) && v.isVisible(inner);
2578   }
2579
2580   /**
2581    * Shortcut for calling <c>Class.getDeclaredConstructor().newInstance()</c> on the underlying class.
2582    *
2583    * @return A new instance of the underlying class
2584    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
2585    */
2586   public Object newInstance() throws ExecutableException {
2587      if (inner == null)
2588         throw exex("Type ''{0}'' cannot be instantiated", getNameFull());
2589      try {
2590         return inner.getDeclaredConstructor().newInstance();
2591      } catch (InvocationTargetException | NoSuchMethodException | InstantiationException | IllegalAccessException e) {
2592         throw exex(e);
2593      }
2594   }
2595
2596   /**
2597    * Returns a detailed string representation of this class.
2598    *
2599    * <p>
2600    * The returned string includes:
2601    * <ul>
2602    *    <li>Modifiers (public, private, protected, static, final, abstract, etc.)
2603    *    <li>Class type (class, interface, enum, annotation, or record)
2604    *    <li>Fully qualified class name
2605    *    <li>Generic type parameters with bounds (if any)
2606    * </ul>
2607    *
2608    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
2609    * <p class='bjava'>
2610    *    <jc>// Regular class</jc>
2611    *    ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>).toString();
2612    *    <jc>// Returns: "public final class java.lang.String"</jc>
2613    *
2614    *    <jc>// Interface</jc>
2615    *    ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(List.<jk>class</jk>).toString();
2616    *    <jc>// Returns: "public interface java.util.List"</jc>
2617    *
2618    *    <jc>// Generic class</jc>
2619    *    ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(ArrayList.<jk>class</jk>).toString();
2620    *    <jc>// Returns: "public class java.util.ArrayList&lt;E&gt;"</jc>
2621    *
2622    *    <jc>// Generic class with bounds</jc>
2623    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(Comparable.<jk>class</jk>);
2624    *    <jv>ci</jv>.toString();
2625    *    <jc>// Returns: "public interface java.lang.Comparable&lt;T&gt;"</jc>
2626    *
2627    *    <jc>// Enum</jc>
2628    *    ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(Month.<jk>class</jk>).toString();
2629    *    <jc>// Returns: "public enum java.time.Month"</jc>
2630    *
2631    *    <jc>// Record</jc>
2632    *    ClassInfo <jv>record</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyRecord.<jk>class</jk>);
2633    *    <jv>record</jv>.toString();
2634    *    <jc>// Returns: "public record org.example.MyRecord"</jc>
2635    * </p>
2636    *
2637    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5>
2638    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
2639    *    <li>For interfaces, the "abstract" modifier is omitted since it's implicit.
2640    *    <li>For non-class types (e.g., TypeVariable, ParameterizedType), returns the result of {@link Type#toString()}.
2641    *    <li>Type parameters include bounds when present (e.g., "&lt;T extends Comparable&lt;T&gt;&gt;").
2642    * </ul>
2643    *
2644    * <h5 class='section'>Comparison with Other Methods:</h5>
2645    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
2646    *    <li>{@link #getNameSimple()} - Returns simple class name without package (e.g., "String")
2647    *    <li>{@link #getNameShort()} - Returns short name with outer classes (e.g., "MyClass$Inner")
2648    *    <li>{@link #getNameFull()} - Returns fully qualified name with type parameters (e.g., "java.util.List&lt;String&gt;")
2649    * </ul>
2650    *
2651    * @return A detailed string representation including modifiers, class type, name, and type parameters.
2652    */
2653   @Override
2654   public String toString() {
2655      return toString.get();
2656   }
2657
2658   @SuppressWarnings({
2659      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for toString method finding
2660   })
2661   private String findToString() {
2662      var sb = new StringBuilder(256);
2663
2664      // Class type (class, interface, enum, annotation, record)
2665      if (inner == null) {
2666         // For non-class types (e.g., TypeVariable, ParameterizedType), use innerType.toString()
2667         return innerType.toString();
2668      }
2669
2670      // Modifiers (filter out implicit modifiers)
2671      var mods = Modifier.toString(getModifiers());
2672      if (nn(mods) && ! mods.isEmpty()) {
2673         // Remove "abstract" for interfaces since it's implicit and redundant
2674         // Remove "final" for enums and records since it's implicit and redundant
2675         // Also remove "interface" if it somehow got included (shouldn't happen, but be safe)
2676         if (isInterface()) {
2677            mods = mods.replace("abstract", "").replace("interface", "").trim().replaceAll("\\s+", " ");
2678         } else if (isEnum() || isRecord()) {
2679            mods = mods.replace("final", "").trim().replaceAll("\\s+", " ");
2680         }
2681         if (! mods.isEmpty()) {
2682            sb.append(mods).append(" ");
2683         }
2684      }
2685
2686      if (isRecord()) {
2687         sb.append("record ");
2688      } else if (isAnnotation()) {
2689         sb.append("@interface ");
2690      } else if (isEnum()) {
2691         sb.append("enum ");
2692      } else if (isInterface()) {
2693         sb.append("interface ");
2694      } else {
2695         sb.append("class ");
2696      }
2697
2698      // Full name
2699      appendNameFormatted(sb, FULL, false, '$', BRACKETS);
2700
2701      // Type parameters (if declared on the class itself, not as a parameterized type)
2702      var typeParams = getTypeParameters();
2703      if (! typeParams.isEmpty()) {
2704         sb.append('<');
2705         var first = true;
2706         for (var tv : typeParams) {
2707            if (! first)
2708               sb.append(", ");
2709            first = false;
2710            sb.append(tv.getName());
2711            var bounds = tv.getBounds();
2712            if (bounds.length > 0 && (bounds.length > 1 || ! bounds[0].equals(Object.class))) {
2713               sb.append(" extends ");
2714               var firstBound = true;
2715               for (var bound : bounds) {
2716                  if (! firstBound)
2717                     sb.append(" & ");
2718                  firstBound = false;
2719                  if (bound instanceof Class<?> boundClass) {
2720                     of(boundClass).appendNameFormatted(sb, FULL, true, '$', BRACKETS);
2721                  } else {
2722                     sb.append(bound.getTypeName());
2723                  }
2724               }
2725            }
2726         }
2727         sb.append('>');
2728      }
2729
2730      return sb.toString();
2731   }
2732
2733   /**
2734    * Unwrap this class if it's a parameterized type of the specified type such as {@link Value} or {@link Optional}.
2735    *
2736    * @param wrapperTypes The parameterized types to unwrap if this class is one of those types.
2737    * @return The class info on the unwrapped type, or just this type if this isn't one of the specified types.
2738    */
2739   public ClassInfo unwrap(Class<?>...wrapperTypes) {
2740      for (var wt : wrapperTypes) {
2741         if (isParameterizedTypeOf(wt)) {
2742            Type t = getFirstParameterType(wt);
2743            if (nn(t))
2744               return of(t).unwrap(wrapperTypes); // Recursively do it again.
2745         }
2746      }
2747      return this;
2748   }
2749
2750   /**
2751    * Helper method to recursively add an interface and its parent interfaces to the set.
2752    *
2753    * @param set The set to add to.
2754    * @param iface The interface to add.
2755    */
2756   private void addInterfaceHierarchy(LinkedHashSet<ClassInfo> set, ClassInfo iface) {
2757      if (! set.add(iface))
2758         return;
2759
2760      // Process parent interfaces recursively
2761      var parentInterfaces = iface.getDeclaredInterfaces();
2762      for (var i = 0; i < parentInterfaces.size(); i++)
2763         addInterfaceHierarchy(set, parentInterfaces.get(i));
2764   }
2765
2766   /**
2767    * Finds all annotations on this class and parent classes/interfaces in child-to-parent order.
2768    *
2769    * <p>
2770    * This is similar to {@link AnnotationProvider#xfind(Class)} but without runtime annotations.
2771    *
2772    * <p>
2773    * Order of traversal:
2774    * <ol>
2775    *    <li>Annotations declared on this class
2776    *    <li>Annotations declared on parent classes (child-to-parent order)
2777    *    <li>For each parent class, annotations on interfaces declared on that class (child-to-parent interface hierarchy)
2778    *    <li>Annotations on the package of this class
2779    * </ol>
2780    *
2781    * @return A list of all annotation infos in child-to-parent order.
2782    */
2783   private List<AnnotationInfo<? extends Annotation>> findAnnotations() {
2784      var list = new ArrayList<AnnotationInfo<Annotation>>();
2785
2786      // On all parent classes and interfaces (properly traversed to avoid duplicates)
2787      var parentsAndInterfaces2 = getParentsAndInterfaces();
2788      for (var i = 0; i < parentsAndInterfaces2.size(); i++) {
2789         var ci = parentsAndInterfaces2.get(i);
2790         // Add declared annotations from this class/interface
2791         for (var a : ci.inner().getDeclaredAnnotations())
2792            streamRepeated(a).forEach(a2 -> list.add(ai(ci, a2)));
2793      }
2794
2795      // On the package of this class
2796      var pkg = getPackage();
2797      if (nn(pkg)) {
2798         var pi = PackageInfo.of(pkg.inner());
2799         for (var a : pkg.inner().getAnnotations())
2800            streamRepeated(a).forEach(a2 -> list.add(ai(pi, a2)));
2801      }
2802
2803      return u(list);
2804   }
2805
2806   private ClassInfo findComponentType() {
2807      Type ct = innerType;
2808      var cc = inner;
2809
2810      // Handle GenericArrayType (e.g., List<String>[])
2811      while (ct instanceof GenericArrayType ct2) {
2812         ct = ct2.getGenericComponentType();
2813      }
2814
2815      // Handle regular arrays
2816      while (nn(cc) && cc.isArray()) {
2817         cc = cc.getComponentType();
2818      }
2819
2820      // Return the deepest component type found
2821      if (ct != innerType) {
2822         return of(ct);
2823      } else if (cc != inner) {
2824         return of(cc);
2825      } else {
2826         return this;
2827      }
2828   }
2829
2830   private int findDimensions() {
2831      int d = 0;
2832      Type ct = innerType;
2833
2834      // Handle GenericArrayType (e.g., List<String>[])
2835      while (ct instanceof GenericArrayType ct2) {
2836         d++;
2837         ct = ct2.getGenericComponentType();
2838      }
2839
2840      // Handle regular arrays
2841      var cc = inner;
2842      while (nn(cc) && cc.isArray()) {
2843         d++;
2844         cc = cc.getComponentType();
2845      }
2846
2847      return d;
2848   }
2849
2850   private List<ClassInfo> findParents() {
2851      List<ClassInfo> l = list();
2852      var pc = inner;
2853      while (nn(pc) && pc != Object.class) {
2854         l.add(of(pc));
2855         pc = pc.getSuperclass();
2856      }
2857      return u(l);
2858   }
2859
2860   /**
2861    * Finds all parent classes and interfaces with proper traversal of interface hierarchy.
2862    *
2863    * @return A list of all parent classes and interfaces without duplicates.
2864    */
2865   private List<ClassInfo> findParentsAndInterfaces() {
2866      var set = new LinkedHashSet<ClassInfo>();
2867
2868      // Process all parent classes (includes this class)
2869      var parents2 = getParents();
2870      for (var i = 0; i < parents2.size(); i++) {
2871         var parent = parents2.get(i);
2872         set.add(parent);
2873
2874         // Process interfaces declared on this parent (and their parent interfaces)
2875         var declaredInterfaces2 = parent.getDeclaredInterfaces();
2876         for (var j = 0; j < declaredInterfaces2.size(); j++)
2877            addInterfaceHierarchy(set, declaredInterfaces2.get(j));
2878      }
2879
2880      return u(toList(set));
2881   }
2882
2883   private MethodInfo findRepeatedAnnotationMethod() {
2884      // @formatter:off
2885      return getPublicMethods().stream()
2886         .filter(m -> m.hasName("value"))
2887         .filter(m -> m.getReturnType().isArray())
2888         .filter(m -> {
2889            var rct = m.getReturnType().getComponentType();
2890            if (rct.hasAnnotation(Repeatable.class)) {
2891               return rct.getAnnotations(Repeatable.class).findFirst().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).orElse(null).value().equals(inner);
2892            }
2893            return false;
2894         })
2895         .findFirst()
2896         .orElse(null);
2897      // @formatter:on
2898   }
2899
2900   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2901   // Find methods
2902   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2903
2904   /**
2905    * Returns the first type parameter of this type if it's parameterized (e.g., T in Optional&lt;T&gt;).
2906    *
2907    * @param parameterizedType The expected parameterized class (e.g., Optional.class).
2908    * @return The first type parameter, or <jk>null</jk> if not parameterized or no parameters exist.
2909    */
2910   private Type getFirstParameterType(Class<?> parameterizedType) {
2911      if (innerType instanceof ParameterizedType innerType2) {
2912         var ta = innerType2.getActualTypeArguments();
2913         if (ta.length > 0)
2914            return ta[0];
2915      } else if (innerType instanceof Class<?> innerType3 && innerType3 != parameterizedType && parameterizedType.isAssignableFrom(innerType3)) /* Class that extends Optional<T> */ {
2916         return ClassInfo.of(innerType3).getParameterType(0, parameterizedType);
2917      }
2918      return null;
2919   }
2920
2921   /**
2922    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this type is a parameterized type of the specified class or a subclass of it.
2923    *
2924    * @param c The class to check against (e.g., Optional.class, List.class).
2925    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this is Optional&lt;T&gt; or a subclass like MyOptional extends Optional&lt;String&gt;.
2926    */
2927   private boolean isParameterizedTypeOf(Class<?> c) {
2928      return (innerType instanceof ParameterizedType t2 && t2.getRawType() == c) || (innerType instanceof Class innerType2 && c.isAssignableFrom(innerType2));
2929   }
2930
2931   /**
2932    * Returns a {@link ConstructorInfo} wrapper for the specified raw {@link Constructor} object.
2933    *
2934    * <p>
2935    * This is an internal method used to wrap raw reflection objects into their cached Info wrappers.
2936    * The wrappers provide additional functionality and are cached to avoid creating duplicate objects.
2937    *
2938    * <p>
2939    * This method is called internally when building the lists of constructors (public, declared, etc.)
2940    * and ensures that the same {@link Constructor} object always maps to the same {@link ConstructorInfo} instance.
2941    *
2942    * @param x The raw constructor to wrap.
2943    * @return The cached {@link ConstructorInfo} wrapper for this constructor.
2944    */
2945   ConstructorInfo getConstructor(Constructor<?> x) {
2946      return constructorCache.get(x, () -> new ConstructorInfo(this, x));
2947   }
2948
2949   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2950   // Annotatable interface methods
2951   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2952
2953   /**
2954    * Returns a {@link FieldInfo} wrapper for the specified raw {@link Field} object.
2955    *
2956    * <p>
2957    * This is an internal method used to wrap raw reflection objects into their cached Info wrappers.
2958    * The wrappers provide additional functionality and are cached to avoid creating duplicate objects.
2959    *
2960    * <p>
2961    * This method is called internally when building the lists of fields (public, declared, all, etc.)
2962    * and ensures that the same {@link Field} object always maps to the same {@link FieldInfo} instance.
2963    *
2964    * @param x The raw field to wrap.
2965    * @return The cached {@link FieldInfo} wrapper for this field.
2966    */
2967   FieldInfo getField(Field x) {
2968      return fieldCache.get(x, () -> new FieldInfo(this, x));
2969   }
2970
2971   /**
2972    * Returns a {@link MethodInfo} wrapper for the specified raw {@link Method} object.
2973    *
2974    * <p>
2975    * This is an internal method used to wrap raw reflection objects into their cached Info wrappers.
2976    * The wrappers provide additional functionality and are cached to avoid creating duplicate objects.
2977    *
2978    * <p>
2979    * This method is called internally when building the lists of methods (public, declared, all, etc.)
2980    * and ensures that the same {@link Method} object always maps to the same {@link MethodInfo} instance.
2981    *
2982    * @param x The raw method to wrap.
2983    * @return The cached {@link MethodInfo} wrapper for this method.
2984    */
2985   MethodInfo getMethod(Method x) {
2986      return methodCache.get(x, () -> new MethodInfo(eq(this.inner, x.getDeclaringClass()) ? this : of(x.getDeclaringClass()), x));
2987   }
2988
2989   /**
2990    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this type is a collection type that should be populated with multiple beans.
2991    *
2992    * <p>
2993    * Supported collection types:
2994    * <ul>
2995    *    <li>Arrays (<c>T[]</c>)
2996    *    <li><c>List&lt;T&gt;</c> (interface only, not concrete implementations like <c>ArrayList</c> or <c>LinkedList</c>)
2997    *    <li><c>Set&lt;T&gt;</c> (interface only, not concrete implementations like <c>HashSet</c> or <c>LinkedHashSet</c>)
2998    *    <li><c>Map&lt;String,T&gt;</c> (interface only, not concrete implementations like <c>HashMap</c> or <c>LinkedHashMap</c>)
2999    * </ul>
3000    *
3001    * <p>
3002    * <b>Note:</b> This matches Spring's behavior where only the base interfaces trigger automatic collection injection.
3003    * Concrete implementations like <c>ArrayList&lt;T&gt;</c> are treated as regular bean types, not collection types.
3004    *
3005    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this type is a supported collection, array, or map type.
3006    */
3007   public boolean isInjectCollectionType() {
3008      if (isArray())
3009         return true;
3010      var inner2 = o(this.inner()).orElse(Object.class);
3011      // Only match the exact interfaces, not their implementations (matches Spring's behavior)
3012      return eq(inner2, List.class) || eq(inner2, Set.class) || eq(inner2, Map.class);
3013   }
3014
3015   /**
3016    * Injects beans from the bean store into the specified object's fields and methods.
3017    *
3018    * <p>
3019    * This method performs field and method injection based on {@code @Inject} or {@code @Autowired} annotations.
3020    * After all injection is complete, it calls any methods annotated with {@code @PostConstruct}.
3021    *
3022    * <h5 class='section'>Injectable Fields:</h5>
3023    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
3024    *    <li>Must be annotated with {@code @Inject} or {@code @Autowired} (matched by simple class name)
3025    *    <li>Must not be final
3026    *    <li>May have any valid name
3027    *    <li>Supports the same parameter types as parameter resolution: single beans, {@code Optional}, arrays, {@code List}, {@code Set}, {@code Map}
3028    * </ul>
3029    *
3030    * <h5 class='section'>Injectable Methods:</h5>
3031    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
3032    *    <li>Must be annotated with {@code @Inject} or {@code @Autowired} (matched by simple class name)
3033    *    <li>Must not be abstract
3034    *    <li>Must not declare type parameters
3035    *    <li>May return any type (return value is ignored)
3036    *    <li>May have any valid name
3037    *    <li>May accept zero or more dependencies as arguments
3038    *    <li>Supports the same parameter types as parameter resolution: single beans, {@code Optional}, arrays, {@code List}, {@code Set}, {@code Map}
3039    * </ul>
3040    *
3041    * <h5 class='section'>PostConstruct Methods:</h5>
3042    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
3043    *    <li>Must be annotated with {@code @PostConstruct} (matched by simple class name)
3044    *    <li>Must return {@code void}
3045    *    <li>Must have no parameters
3046    *    <li>Must not be abstract
3047    *    <li>Must not declare type parameters
3048    *    <li>Called after all field and method injection is complete
3049    *    <li>Called in parent-to-child order (parent methods execute before child methods)
3050    *    <li>Multiple methods can be annotated, and all will be called in order
3051    * </ul>
3052    *
3053    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3054    * <p class='bjava'>
3055    *    <jc>// Class with injectable fields and methods</jc>
3056    *    <jk>public</jk> <jk>class</jk> MyService {
3057    *       <ja>@Inject</ja>
3058    *       <jk>private</jk> MyDependency <jv>dependency</jv>;
3059    *
3060    *       <ja>@Autowired</ja>
3061    *       <jk>private</jk> List&lt;MyService&gt; <jv>allServices</jv>;
3062    *
3063    *       <ja>@Inject</ja>
3064    *       <jk>public</jk> <jk>void</jk> init(MyConfig <jv>config</jv>) {
3065    *          <jc>// Initialization logic</jc>
3066    *       }
3067    *
3068    *       <ja>@PostConstruct</ja>
3069    *       <jk>public</jk> <jk>void</jk> postConstruct() {
3070    *          <jc>// Called after all injection is complete</jc>
3071    *       }
3072    *    }
3073    *
3074    *    <jc>// Inject beans</jc>
3075    *    MyService <jv>service</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyService();
3076    *    ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>service</jv>).<jsm>inject</jsm>(<jv>beanStore</jv>);
3077    * </p>
3078    *
3079    * @param <T> The bean type.
3080    * @param bean The object to inject beans into.
3081    * @param beanStore The bean store to resolve beans from.
3082    * @return The same bean instance (for method chaining).
3083    * @throws ExecutableException If any field or method cannot be injected (e.g., required bean not found in the bean store).
3084    */
3085   public <T> T inject(T bean, BeanStore beanStore) {
3086      // Inject into fields.
3087      getAllFields().stream()
3088         .filter(x -> x.isNotFinal() && x.getAnnotations().stream().anyMatch(ClassInfo::isInjectOrValueAnnotation))
3089         .forEach(x -> x.inject(beanStore, bean));
3090
3091      // Inject into methods.
3092      getAllMethods().stream()
3093      .filter(x -> x.isNotAbstract() && eq(x.getTypeParameters().length, 0) && x.getAnnotations().stream().anyMatch(ClassInfo::isInjectOrValueAnnotation))
3094         .forEach(x -> x.inject(beanStore, bean));
3095
3096      // Call @PostConstruct methods after all injection is complete
3097      // Use getAllMethodsTopDown() to ensure parent methods are called before child methods
3098      getAllMethodsTopDown().stream()
3099         .filter(x -> x.isNotAbstract() && eq(x.getTypeParameters().length, 0))
3100         .filter(x -> x.getReturnType().is(void.class) && eq(x.getParameterCount(), 0))
3101         .filter(x -> x.getAnnotations().stream().anyMatch(JsrSupport::isPostConstructAnnotation))
3102         .forEach(x -> x.inject(beanStore, bean));
3103
3104      return bean;
3105   }
3106
3107   private static boolean isInjectOrValueAnnotation(AnnotationInfo<?> ai) {
3108      return JsrSupport.isInjectAnnotation(ai) || JsrSupport.isValueAnnotation(ai);
3109   }
3110
3111}
